details.name: Formula 1
details.description:
The FIA Formula 1 World Championship (F1) is the premier international single-seater auto racing series, organized by the Fédération Internationale de l’Automobile (FIA). The series features a global calendar of races, known as Grands Prix, held on various circuits around the world, ranging from iconic tracks like Monaco to modern circuits like Yas Marina in Abu Dhabi.

The series consists of a single class of hybrid powered single seaters designed and built by the teams themselves The championship includes a diverse range of drivers from around the world, all aiming to achieve the ultimate accolade in motorsport: the title of Formula 1 World Champion.

details.name: Formula 2
details.description:
The FIA Formula 2 Championship (F2) is a premier international single-seater racing series and a key feeder to Formula 1, organized by the Fédération Internationale de l’Automobile (FIA). The series follows a global calendar, often supporting Formula 1 Grands Prix, allowing drivers to compete on renowned circuits worldwide.

In F2, all teams race with identical Dallara-built cars powered by Mecachrome engines, emphasizing driver skill and team strategy. This series serves as a crucial step for aspiring Formula 1 drivers, offering intense competition and the chance to impress F1 teams and scouts. The championship features a mix of emerging talents and seasoned racers, all striving for the title of Formula 2 Champion.

details.name: Formula 3
details.description:
The FIA Formula 3 Championship (F3) is an international single-seater racing series, serving as a stepping stone to higher tiers like Formula 2 and Formula 1, organized by the Fédération Internationale de l’Automobile (FIA). The series features a global calendar, often supporting Formula 1 Grands Prix, allowing young drivers to race on some of the world’s most famous circuits.

In F3, all teams compete with identical Dallara-built cars powered by Mecachrome engines, highlighting driver talent and team strategy. This series is crucial for aspiring racers aiming to advance their careers, offering intense competition and the opportunity to gain experience in front of F1 teams and scouts. The championship showcases emerging talents, all aiming for the title of Formula 3 Champion.

details.name: F1 Academy
details.description:
The F1 Academy is an international single-seater racing series designed to nurture and develop young female drivers, organized by the Fédération Internationale de l’Automobile (FIA). The series features a global calendar, often supporting Formula 1 Grands Prix, providing a platform for emerging talents to compete on some of the world’s most prestigious circuits.

In the F1 Academy, all teams race with identical cars, ensuring that driver skill and team strategy are paramount. This series plays a vital role in promoting diversity in motorsport, offering intense competition and valuable experience for young female drivers aiming to advance their careers. The championship showcases the next generation of talent, all striving for success and recognition in the world of motorsport.

details.name: Formula E
details.description:
The FIA Formula E Championship is an international single-seater electric racing series, organized by the Fédération Internationale de l’Automobile (FIA). The series features a global calendar of races, often held on street circuits in major cities, highlighting the importance of sustainability and innovation in motorsport.

In Formula E, all teams compete with identical electric cars, emphasizing driver skill and team strategy while promoting the development of electric vehicle technology. This series serves as a platform for leading automotive manufacturers and new talent, offering intense competition and the opportunity to showcase advancements in electric mobility. The championship includes a diverse range of drivers and teams, all vying for the title of Formula E Champion and contributing to the future of sustainable racing.

details.name: IndyCar
details.description:
The NTT IndyCar Series is a premier American single-seater racing series, organized by INDYCAR. The series features a diverse calendar of races, including street circuits, road courses, and oval tracks, showcasing the versatility and skill of the drivers.

In IndyCar, all teams compete with standardized Dallara chassis and engines supplied by Honda or Chevrolet, emphasizing driver talent and team strategy. This series is renowned for its close, competitive racing and the prestigious Indianapolis 500, one of the most famous motorsport events in the world. The championship attracts a mix of experienced drivers and rising stars, all striving for the title of IndyCar Champion and the glory that comes with winning in one of the most demanding and exciting racing series.

details.name: Indy NXT
details.description:
The Indy NXT by Firestone series is a premier American developmental single-seater racing series, organized by INDYCAR. The series follows a diverse calendar of races, including street circuits, road courses, and oval tracks, providing a crucial platform for aspiring drivers to develop their skills.

In Indy NXT, all teams compete with identical Dallara chassis and engines, ensuring that driver talent and team strategy are paramount. This series serves as a stepping stone to the NTT IndyCar Series, offering intense competition and the opportunity for young drivers to gain valuable experience. The championship features a mix of emerging talents, all striving for the title of Indy NXT Champion and aiming to progress to the top tier of American open-wheel racing.

details.name: FIA WEC
details.description:
The FIA World Endurance Championship (WEC) is an international sportscar series run by the French organisers, the ACO. The series consists of endurace races, ranging from 6 to 24 hours, and includes the prestegious Le Mans 24 Hours.

Currently the series runs 2 classes. The Hypercar class and features manufacturer produced prototypes, whilst the LMGT3 class consists of GT3 spec cars and has a Pro-Am driver setup.

details.name: IMSA WeatherTech
details.description:
The IMSA WeatherTech SportsCar Championship is a premier North American sportscar racing series organized by the International Motor Sports Association (IMSA). The series features endurance races, ranging from 2.4 hours to 24 hours, and includes the prestigious Rolex 24 at Daytona. Currently, the series runs multiple classes, with the GTP (Grand Touring Prototype) as the top tier, featuring manufacturer-produced hybrid prototypes. The LMP2 (Le Mans Prototype 2) class caters to privateer teams using spec chassis and engines, while the LMP3 (Le Mans Prototype 3) serves as an entry-level prototype class aimed at developing drivers and teams.

In addition to the prototype classes, the GT Daytona Pro (GTD Pro) class consists of GT3-spec cars with professional drivers, and the GT Daytona (GTD) class follows a similar format but with a Pro-Am driver setup, combining professional and amateur drivers. These classes ensure a diverse and competitive field, showcasing both cutting-edge technology and driver skill across a range of world-class circuits.

details.name: European Le Mans Series
details.description:
The European Le Mans Series (ELMS) is a prominent European sportscar racing series organized by the Automobile Club de l’Ouest (ACO). The series features endurance races, typically ranging from 4 to 6 hours, and serves as a stepping stone to the FIA WEC. Currently, the series runs several classes, including LMP2 (Le Mans Prototype 2), which is the top tier and features spec chassis and engines for privateer teams. The LMP3 (Le Mans Prototype 3) class is an entry-level prototype category aimed at developing drivers and teams.

In addition to the prototype classes, the ELMS also includes the LMGT3 (Le Mans Grand Touring Endurance) class, which consists of GT3-spec cars and a Pro-Am driver setup, combining professional and amateur drivers. These classes ensure a diverse and competitive field, showcasing both advanced technology and driver skill across a variety of iconic European circuits. Successful teams in ELMS receive invites to compete in the 24 Hours of Le Mans.

details.name: Asian Le Mans Series
details.description:
The Asian Le Mans Series is a premier sportscar racing series in Asia organized by the Automobile Club de l’Ouest (ACO). The series features endurance races, typically ranging from 4 to 6 hours, and provides a pathway for teams and drivers aiming to compete in the prestigious 24 Hours of Le Mans. Currently, the series includes several classes, with LMP2 (Le Mans Prototype 2) as the top tier, featuring spec chassis and engines for privateer teams. The LMP3 (Le Mans Prototype 3) class serves as an entry-level prototype category, fostering the development of drivers and teams.

In addition to the prototype classes, the Asian Le Mans Series also includes the GT class, which consists of GT3-spec cars with a Pro-Am driver setup, blending professional and amateur drivers. These classes ensure a diverse and competitive field, highlighting both advanced technology and driver skill across a variety of renowned circuits throughout Asia.

details.name: IMSA Pilot
details.description:
The IMSA Michelin Pilot Challenge is a premier North American sportscar racing series organized by the International Motor Sports Association (IMSA). The series features endurance races, typically ranging from 2 to 4 hours, and is known for its competitive and diverse field. The series consists of two main classes: the Grand Sport (GS) class and the Touring Car (TCR) class. The GS class features GT4-spec cars, which are based on production models but heavily modified for racing, while the TCR class includes front-wheel-drive, turbocharged touring cars, also based on production models.

This multi-class structure ensures a dynamic and thrilling racing experience, showcasing a range of car manufacturers and racing technologies. The Pro-Am driver setup in both classes combines professional and amateur drivers, emphasizing both driver skill and team strategy. The IMSA Michelin Pilot Challenge races on some of North America’s most iconic circuits, providing a platform for up-and-coming talent and seasoned racers alike.

details.name: Le Mans Cup
details.description:
The Michelin Le Mans Cup is a prestigious European sportscar racing series organized by the Automobile Club de l’Ouest (ACO). The series features endurance races, typically ranging from 2 to 4 hours, and serves as a supportive platform to the European Le Mans Series. The Michelin Le Mans Cup runs two main classes: the LMP3 (Le Mans Prototype 3) class and the GT3 class. The LMP3 class is designed for entry-level prototypes, offering teams and drivers a chance to develop their skills, while the GT3 class consists of GT3-spec cars based on production models, providing a diverse and competitive field.

Both classes operate with a Pro-Am driver setup, combining professional and amateur drivers to emphasize teamwork and strategy. The Michelin Le Mans Cup races on some of Europe’s most renowned circuits, offering a mix of challenging tracks that test both driver ability and vehicle performance. This series plays a crucial role in the development of future talent and provides exciting racing action for teams, drivers, and fans alike.

details.name: IMSA SportsCar Challenge
details.description:
The IMSA VP Racing SportsCar Challenge is an exciting North American racing series organized by the International Motor Sports Association (IMSA). The series features sprint races, typically lasting around 45 minutes, offering intense and fast-paced competition. The IMSA VP Racing SportsCar Challenge includes two primary classes: the LMP3 (Le Mans Prototype 3) class and the GSX class. The LMP3 class showcases entry-level prototypes, allowing teams and drivers to hone their skills in a highly competitive environment. The GSX class features GT4-spec cars, which are based on production models but modified for racing.

This multi-class format ensures a dynamic racing experience, highlighting a range of car manufacturers and advanced racing technologies. The series emphasizes driver development, making it an excellent platform for emerging talent and seasoned racers alike. Competing on some of North America’s most iconic circuits, the IMSA VP Racing SportsCar Challenge provides thrilling racing action and serves as a vital stepping stone for those aiming to progress to higher levels of motorsport.

details.name: IGTC
details.description:
The Intercontinental GT Challenge (IGTC) is a global sportscar racing series organized by SRO Motorsports Group. The series features endurance races on some of the world’s most iconic circuits, including the Bathurst 12 Hour and the Spa 24 Hours. The IGTC primarily includes GT3-spec cars, showcasing a diverse field of manufacturer-backed teams and top-tier drivers.

With a focus on international competition, the IGTC emphasizes racing action and a diverse grid of cars. Class range from fully professional line-ups, down to various combinations of pro and amateur drivers. Many of the events in the IGTC calendar form part of other series, such as GTWC Endurance and GTWC America,

details.name: NLS (VLN) / N24
details.description:
The Nürburgring Endurance Series (NLS) is a racing series held at the legendary Nürburgring Nordschleife in Germany. NLS races last between 4 and 6 hours and have massive grids of cars - often over 100 entries. The top class of cars are GT3 spec, but other classes include GT4 and single make classes such as Porsche Carrera Cup, BMW M2 CS and M240i, TCR and even smaller road adapted hatchbacks.

NLS does not technically include the Nurburgring 24 Hours, but many teams that complete at the N24 will also compete in NLS. For simplicities sake, The Racing Line includes the Nurburgring 24 in the NLS caledar.

details.name: Super Formula
details.description:
Super Formula is Japan’s premier single-seater racing series, featuring state-of-the-art Dallara SF19 chassis and turbocharged engines. Standards in Super Formula are high, attracting European single seater drivers looking for an alternative route to Formula 1. 

The single make cars are designed to improve racing and having less problems with cars following in dirty air.

details.name: Super GT
details.description:
Super GT is Japan’s premier GT series, known for its highly competitive field and technical diversity. The series features two main classes: GT500 and GT300. GT500 cars are heavily modified, high-performance vehicles developed by top manufacturers like Nissan, Honda, and Toyota, featuring advanced aerodynamics and cutting-edge technology. Laptimes produced by the GT500 rival the previous generation LMP1 cars.

GT300 cars are a mix of GT3-spec and other specially developed vehicles, providing a diverse and competitive racing environment.

details.name: Super Taikyu
details.description:
The Super Taikyu Series is one of Japan’s leading endurance racing championships, blending professional competition with a strong grassroots spirit. Races typically run from three to five hours, with the headline Fuji 24 Hours standing as one of Asia’s premier round-the-clock events. Contested at iconic circuits such as Fuji Speedway and Suzuka Circuit, the series demands strategy, consistency, and teamwork, with multiple drivers sharing each car across long-distance races.

A defining feature of Super Taikyu is its diverse, multi-class grid, ranging from GT3 machinery in the top ST-X class to production-based cars in the lower categories. This creates constant on-track traffic and strategic complexity, as vastly different cars compete simultaneously. With a mix of factory-backed teams, experienced professionals, and amateur drivers, the series offers a unique blend of accessibility and high-level racing, making it a key part of Japan’s motorsport landscape.

details.name: Super Formula Lights
details.description:
Super Formula Lights is Japan’s premier single-seater feeder series, serving as a crucial stepping stone for drivers aiming to progress to the Super Formula Championship. The series utilizes the advanced Dallara 324 chassis, introduced in 2024, which features updated aerodynamics and incorporates the Halo safety device. Powering these machines is a spec Toyota TGE33 engine developed by TOM’S, a turbocharged three-cylinder 1.6-liter unit derived from the Toyota GR Yaris, producing approximately 275 horsepower.  This combination offers a balanced platform that emphasizes driver skill and promotes close, competitive racing.

As a direct feeder to Super Formula, Super Formula Lights maintains high standards, attracting both domestic talents and international drivers seeking alternative pathways to top-tier motorsport. The series’ alignment with Super Formula ensures that drivers gain relevant experience, making it an ideal environment for those aiming to ascend the motorsport ladder.

details.name: DTM
details.description: The Deutsche Tourenwagen Masters (DTM) is Germany’s premier touring car racing series. Currently utilising GT3 spec cars, it hosts 2 races per weekend and includes unique circuits not found anywhere else, such as the Norisring.

details.name: FR Euro Champ
details.description:
The Formula Regional European Championship by Alpine (FRECA) is a highly regarded single-seater racing series in Europe. Serving as a crucial step for young drivers aiming to progress to higher levels of motorsport, FRECA features races on prominent circuits such as Monza, Spa-Francorchamps, and the Red Bull Ring.

The series typically hosts multiple races per weekend, allowing drivers ample opportunity to showcase their skills and gain valuable experience. FRECA attracts a competitive field of emerging talent from around the world, making it a vital platform for driver development and career progression in motorsport.

details.name: FR America
details.description:
The Formula Regional Americas Championship is a key single-seater racing series in North America, designed to develop young drivers aiming for higher tiers of motorsport. Competing on well-known circuits such as Road America, Circuit of the Americas, and Watkins Glen, the series provides a rigorous testing ground for emerging talent.

Each race weekend typically features multiple races, giving drivers ample track time to hone their skills and gain experience. The championship attracts a diverse field of competitors, making it an important stepping stone for those looking to advance their careers in motorsport.

details.name: FR Japan
details.description:
The Formula Regional Japanese Championship is a prominent single-seater racing series in Japan, aimed at developing young drivers aspiring to reach higher levels of motorsport. The series races on iconic Japanese circuits such as Suzuka, Fuji Speedway, and Twin Ring Motegi, providing a challenging and diverse racing environment.

Each race weekend typically includes multiple races, offering drivers extensive track time.. The championship attracts a mix of domestic and international talent, making it a crucial platform for driver development and progression within the motorsport ladder in asia.

details.name: FR Middle East
details.description:
The Formula Regional Middle East Championship is a significant single-seater racing series in the Middle East, designed to nurture young drivers aiming for higher levels of motorsport. The series competes on renowned circuits such as Yas Marina, Dubai Autodrome, and Kuwait Motor Town, providing a diverse and challenging racing experience.

Each race weekend typically features multiple races, allowing drivers ample opportunity to develop their skills and gain valuable track experience. The championship draws a competitive field of emerging talent from around the world, making it an essential platform for driver development and career advancement in motorsport.

details.name: FR Oceania
details.description:
The Formula Regional Oceanic Championship is a key single-seater racing series in the Oceanic region, focused on developing young drivers aiming for higher levels of motorsport. The series competes on prominent circuits such as Phillip Island, Sydney Motorsport Park, and Hampton Downs, offering a variety of challenging tracks.

Each race weekend typically includes multiple races, providing drivers with extensive track time to hone their skills and gain competitive experience. The championship attracts a diverse field of emerging talent from the region and beyond, serving as an essential platform for driver development and progression within the motorsport ladder.

details.name: GTWC Europe
details.description:
The GT World Challenge (GTWC) Endurance Cup is a premier grand touring car racing series organized by SRO Motorsports Group. The series features 3 hour endurance races and 1 hour sprint held throughout Europe. The legendary Spa 24 hours is currently part of the calendar.

Cars are GT3 spec, but Pro and various Pro Am classes are part of the events. Pit stop strategy and driver changes play large roles in the outcome of races.

details.name: GTWC America
details.description:
The GT World Challenge America (GTWC America) is an international sportscar series organized by SRO Motorsports Group. The series features races that tend to be around 90 minutes long, but the calendar also includes the Indianapolis 8 Hours as part of the IGTC Series..

Currently, the series runs two main classes. The Pro class features manufacturer-supported teams and professional drivers, while the Pro-Am class consists of GT3 spec cars with a mix of professional and amateur drivers.

details.name: GTWC Australia
details.description:
The GT World Challenge Australia (GTWC Australia) is an international sportscar series organized by SRO Motorsports Group. The series features endurance races across various iconic Australian circuits.

Currently, the series runs two main classes. The Pro class features manufacturer-supported teams and professional drivers, while the Pro-Am class consists of GT3 spec cars with a mix of professional and amateur drivers.

details.name: GTWC Asia
details.description:
The GT World Challenge Asia (GTWC Asia) is an international sportscar series organized by SRO Motorsports Group. The series features endurance races across various renowned circuits throughout Asia.

Currently, the series runs two main classes. The Pro class features manufacturer-supported teams and professional drivers, while the Pro-Am class consists of GT3 spec cars with a mix of professional and amateur drivers.

details.name: Japan Cup
details.description:
The Japan Cup, part of the GT World Challenge Asia (GTWC Asia), is an international sportscar series organized by SRO Motorsports Group. The series features endurance races held exclusively at renowned circuits throughout Japan.

Currently, the series runs two main classes. The Pro class features manufacturer-supported teams and professional drivers, while the Pro-Am class consists of GT3 spec cars with a mix of professional and amateur drivers.

details.name: SRO GT Cup
details.description:
The SRO GT Cup is a championship organized by SRO Motorsports Group, featuring GT4-spec cars competing across China and Asia. The series provides a platform for both professional and amateur drivers to race production-based sports cars that comply with SRO’s GT4 regulations, designed to ensure competitive balance through the Balance of Performance (BoP) system.

The championship showcases a variety of manufacturers and teams, offering accessible and closely matched racing within a professional environment. By combining cost-effective competition with the prestige of GT racing, the SRO GT Cup plays an important role in developing regional motorsport and providing a pathway for drivers aiming to progress within the global GT racing structure.

details.name: International GT Open
details.description:
The International GT Open is a premier sportscar series organized by GT Sport. The series features endurance races across various prestigious circuits in Europe.

Currently, the series runs two main classes. The Pro class features manufacturer-supported teams and professional drivers, while the Pro-Am class consists of GT3 spec cars with a mix of professional and amateur drivers.

details.name: British GT
details.description:
The British GT Championship is a premier sportscar series organized by the SRO. The series features a mix of endurance and sprint races, with race lengths varying from one-hour sprints to three-hour endurance events, held across various circuits in the United Kingdom.

Currently, the series runs two main classes. The GT3 class features manufacturer-supported teams and professional drivers, while the GT4 class consists of GT4 spec cars with a mix of professional and amateur drivers.

details.name: Euroformula Open
details.description:
The Euroformula Open is a prestigious single-seater racing series organized by GT Sport. The series features races across various renowned circuits in Europe, offering a platform for emerging talents to showcase their skills.

The race lengths are around 40 minutes, with events held over a weekend featuring multiple races.

details.name: GT Cup Open Europe
details.description:
The GT Cup Open Europe (GTC Open) is a prominent sportscar series organized by GT Sport. The series features races across various circuits in Europe, offering a competitive platform for GT cars.

The race lengths are around 50 minutes and includetwo races a weekend. The series utilises cars which are traditionally single make series cars - Ferrari Challenge and Porsche Cup cars.

details.name: GT2 Europe
details.description:
The GT2 European Series is an international sportscar series organized by the SRO Motorsports Group. The series consists of endurance races, typically with 2x 1 hour races per weekend. 

GT2 is largely for amateur drivers and has 2 drivers per car, with a driver change. The cars have more power than their GT3 equivalents, with less driver aids and less downforce, making them a challenge to drive.

details.name: GT4 Europe
details.description:
The GT4 European Series is an international sportscar series organized by the SRO Motorsports Group. The series consists of endurance races, typically featuring two 1-hour races per weekend.

GT4 is designed primarily for amateur drivers and features two drivers per car, with a mandatory driver change. The cars are closely based on their road-going counterparts, making them more affordable and accessible for drivers. They have less power and more driver aids compared to their GT3 equivalents, providing an excellent platform for learning and competitive racing.

details.name: GT America
details.description:
The GT America Series is an international sportscar series organized by the SRO Motorsports Group. The series consists of sprint races, typically lasting around 40 minutes each, and features some of the most iconic circuits in North America.

GT2, GT3 and GT4 cars are all eligable. Older generation of GT3s are also regularly entered. GT America acts as a feeder series to the larger GT World Challenge America

details.name: GT4 America
details.description:
The GT4 America Series is an international sportscar series organized by the SRO Motorsports Group. The series consists of sprint races, typically lasting around 50 minutes each, and features some of the most iconic circuits in North America.

Currently, the series runs a single class featuring GT4 spec cars. These cars are closely based on their road-going counterparts, making them more affordable and accessible for drivers. The races feature a Pro-Am driver setup, with two drivers per car and a mandatory driver change, ensuring a mix of professional and amateur talent on the grid.

details.name: GT4 Australia
details.description: GT4 Australia, commercially known as Monochrome GT4 Australia, is a premier Australian motorsport series featuring GT4-class grand touring cars. Established in 2024 as a standalone championship, it is managed by the SRO Motorsports Group and showcases a diverse range of manufacturers, including Porsche, McLaren, Mercedes-AMG, and Ford. The series runs in conjunction with the GT World Challenge Australia events, offering competitive racing across Australia’s top circuits.

details.name: GB3
details.description:
The GB3 Championship is a premier single-seater racing series in the United Kingdom, organized by MotorSport Vision. The series consists of sprint races, typically lasting around 20 to 30 minutes each, and features some of the most iconic circuits in the UK.

The GB3 Championship runs a single class featuring high-performance Formula 3 spec cars. These cars are designed to be a stepping stone for drivers aspiring to reach higher levels of motorsport, providing a platform to develop their skills and showcase their talent.

details.name: GB4
details.description:
The GB4 Championship is a premier single-seater racing series in the United Kingdom, organized by MotorSport Vision. The series consists of sprint races, typically lasting around 20 to 30 minutes each, and features some of the most iconic circuits in the UK.

The GB4 Championship runs a single class featuring high-performance Formula 4 spec cars. These cars are designed as an entry-level category for drivers aiming to progress through the ranks of motorsport. The championship provides a platform for young, aspiring drivers to develop their skills and gain valuable racing experience.

details.name: Ultimate Formula Cup
details.description:
The Ultimate Cup Formula Cup is a European single seater championship. Race weekends consist of 3x 20 minute races.

Eligable cars are regional F3, and Formula Renault 2.0 cars.

details.name: Formula Winter Series
details.description:
The Formula Winter Series is a specialized single-seater racing championship held during the winter months, designed to provide valuable track time and experience for young drivers. The series races on renowned circuits in warmer climates, such as those in Spain and Portugal, allowing for consistent racing conditions despite the season.

Formula Winter Series is part of a larger Winter Series event. Each race weekend typically features multiple races, giving drivers ample opportunity to develop their skills and gain competitive experience in a condensed time frame.

details.name: ADAC GT Masters
details.description:
The ADAC GT Masters is a premier sportscar racing series in Germany, organized by the ADAC. The series consists of sprint races, typically featuring two 1-hour races per weekend, and largely takes place in Germany

The ADAC GT Masters runs a single class featuring high-performance GT3 spec cars from various manufacturers. The races have a Pro-Am driver setup, with two drivers per car and a mandatory driver change, ensuring a mix of professional and amateur talent on the grid.

details.name: ADAC GT4 Germany
details.description:
The ADAC GT4 Germany is a premier sportscar racing series in Germany, organized by the ADAC. The series consists of sprint races, typically featuring two 1-hour races per weekend, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits in Germany and across Europe.

The ADAC GT4 Germany runs a single class featuring GT4 spec cars, which are closely based on their road-going counterparts. The series is designed for amateur and semi-professional drivers, providing an accessible entry point into high-level motorsport.

details.name: FFSA GT
details.description:
The FFSA GT4 France is a premier sportscar racing series in France, organized by the FFSA. The series consists of sprint races, typically featuring two 1-hour races per weekend, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits in France and across Europe.

The FFSA GT4 France runs a single class featuring GT4 spec cars, which are closely based on their road-going counterparts. The series is designed for amateur and semi-professional drivers, providing an accessible entry point into high-level motorsport.

details.name: FFSA Tourisme
details.description:
The FFSA Tourisme is a premier touring car racing series in France, organized by the Fédération Française du Sport Automobile (FFSA). The series features sprint races, typically comprising multiple short-duration races per weekend, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits in France and occasionally across Europe.

The FFSA Tourisme runs multiple classes featuring touring cars that are closely based on their road-going counterparts. Designed for amateur and semi-professional drivers, the series provides an accessible entry point into high-level motorsport. It emphasizes close competition and driver development, making it an ideal platform for those looking to advance in touring car racing.

details.name: Italian GT
details.description: The Italian GT Championship (Campionato Italiano Gran Turismo) is Italy’s premier grand tourer racing series, organised by the Automobile Club d’Italia (ACI) and CSAI. The championship, founded in 2003, features a split into Sprint and Endurance cups

details.name: Italian GT Cup
details.description: The Italian GT Cup is a category within the larger Italian GT Championship (Campionato Italiano Gran Turismo), organised by ACI Sport. It complements the top-tier GT3 class and is tailored for one-make cup cars and more accessible GT machinery.

details.name: Italian GT4
details.description: Italian GT4 is Italy’s own GT4 series, acting as a feeder series to Italian GT. It is jointly organised by the Automobile Club d’Italia (ACI) and SRO.

details.name: 24H Series
details.description:
The Creventic 24H Series is an international endurance racing series organised by Creventic. The series consists of endurance races, ranging from 12 to 24 hours, and includes some of the most renowned circuits around the world.

The Creventic 24H Series runs multiple classes, accommodating a wide range of GT and touring cars.  The series is focused on amateur drivers., with teams often consisting of three to five drivers sharing driving duties. Creventic also utilises a unique format at some rounds with split races, where part of the race is run on Saturday and part on Sunday. This makes it accessible to amateur drivers.

details.name: Eurocup-3
details.description:
The Eurocup-3 is a single-seater racing series in Europe. The series consists of sprint races, typically lasting around 20 to 30 minutes each, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits across Europe.

The Eurocup-3 runs a single class featuring high-performance Formula 3 spec cars. These cars are designed to be a crucial stepping stone for drivers aspiring to progress to higher levels of motorsport, providing a platform to develop their skills and showcase their talent.

details.name: E4
details.description:
The Euro4 Championship is a single-seater racing series in Europe. The series consists of sprint races, typically lasting around 20 to 30 minutes each, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits across Europe.

The Euro4 Championship runs a single class featuring high-performance Formula 4 spec cars. These cars are designed as an entry-level category for drivers aiming to progress through the ranks of motorsport, providing a platform for young, aspiring drivers to develop their skills and gain valuable racing experience.

details.name: British Endurance Champ
details.description:
The Britcar Endurance Championship is a premier sportscar racing series in the United Kingdom, organized by Britcar. The series consists of endurance races, typically ranging from 1 to 2 hours, and features some of the most iconic circuits in the UK.

The Britcar Endurance Championship runs multiple classes, accommodating a wide range of GT and touring cars. The races feature a mix of professional and amateur drivers, with teams often consisting of two drivers sharing driving duties.

details.name: Prototype Cup
details.description:
The Prototype Cup is a premier sportscar racing series in Europe, organized by the ADAC and Creventic. The series consists of endurance races, typically ranging from 1 to 2 hours, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits in Europe. 

The Prototype Cup runs a single class featuring LMP3 prototype cars. These cars are designed to be a stepping stone for drivers aiming to progress to higher levels of prototype racing.

details.name: Gulf 12 Hours
details.description:
Launched at the beginning of 2012, the Gulf 12 Hours established itself as a benchmark for GT motorsport events in the Gulf region.

The first nine editions took place at Yas Marina Circuit, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates between 2010 and 2019. Bahrain International Circuit hosted a special edition of the Gulf 12 Hours in January 2021. The Gulf 12 Hours returned at Yas Marina Circuit, Abu Dhabi, for its 10th edition on 8 January 2022.

The 2024 edition of the Gulf 12 Hours will take place at Yas Marina Circuit, Abu Dhabi on Sunday 15 December.

details.name: GT Cup UK
details.description:
The GT Cup Championship UK is a sportscar racing series in the United Kingdom, organized by Bute Motorsport and MSVR. The series consists of a mix of Sprint and Endurance races and is focused on amateur drivers.

GT Cup has multiple GT Classes with a wide range of cars. Eligable car classes include cars based on GT2, GT3, GT4 and GTE cars. Older generation of cars are also often eligable.

details.name: Iberian Supercars
details.description:
Each race weekend in the Iberian Peninsula's popular GT4, TCR, and one-make series cars features two 15-minute qualifying sessions and two 50-minute races with a mandatory pit stop, offering drivers and teams ample track time across practice, qualifying, and races.

The combined calendar of the three series includes six events, held at iconic circuits across Spain and Portugal such as Circuito de Madrid Jarama, Circuito de Jerez, Circuit Ricardo Tormo in Cheste, Autódromo Internacional do Algarve, Circuito do Estoril, and the ultimate challenge — the Vila Real Street Circuit.

The FIA driver categorisation system and Balance of Performance (BoP) for GT classes, along with a single tyre supplier, are key elements behind the success of this racing format.

details.name: GT Cup CEZ
details.description:
The GT Cup CEZ (Central European Zone) is a sports car racing series held across Central and Eastern Europe. It forms part of the ESET Cup race weekends and features a diverse grid of GT cars.

The series includes a mix of Sprint and Endurance-style races and is open to both amateur and semi-professional drivers.

GT Cup CEZ runs multiple classes based on GT2, GT3, GT4, and GTE specifications, with older generations of these cars often eligible to compete. This variety creates close and competitive racing across a wide range of machinery.

details.name: South African Endurance Series
details.description:
The Southern African Endurance Series is a multi-round endurance racing championship held at circuits across Southern Africa. Established in 2011, the series features multiple race classes based on vehicle specifications, with teams competing over races lasting several hours. Points are awarded based on finishing positions and accumulated across the season to determine overall champions. Endurance racing in South Africa dates back to 1956, providing historical context for the series.

The championship currently includes over 200 participants competing across six rounds in seven categories. The annual 9 Hours race at Kyalami Grand Prix Circuit is the flagship event, attracting the largest number of competitors and spectators. In the 2024 season, the series featured entrants from 16 countries. The series is 90% owned by Xolile Letlaka, South Africa’s first Black national circuit racing promoter.

details.name: BTCC
details.description:
The British Touring Car Championship (BTCC) is The UKs premier touring car racing series, organized by TOCA. The series consists of sprint races, typically featuring three 30-minute races per weekend, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits in the UK.

The BTCC runs a single class featuring highly modified touring cars from various manufacturers. The series is known for its close, door-to-door racing and features a mix of professional and amateur drivers. The races often involve intense competition and entertainment elements such as reverse grids and hybrid boost systems, ensuring thrilling and unpredictable racing throughout the season.

details.name: TOCA Junior
details.description:
The TOCA Junior Championship, set to debut in 2025, is a premier motorsport series designed for drivers aged 14 to 17, serving as a vital bridge between karting and professional racing. As a support series to the British Touring Car Championship (BTCC), it offers young talents the opportunity to compete on prominent circuits across the UK, benefiting from substantial trackside audiences and extensive live television coverage on ITV4. 

Central to the championship is the Chevron B1417, a rear-wheel-drive car meticulously crafted with safety and performance in mind. Unveiled in August 2024, the B1417 features a custom-built four-cylinder naturally aspirated 1898cc engine paired with a five-speed manual gearbox.

details.name: Ligier European Series
details.description: The Ligier European Series is a premier racing series in Europe, organized by Ligier Automotive in collaboration with the Automobile Club de l’Ouest (ACO). The series features endurance races, typically comprising two 1-hour races per weekend, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits across Europe, often running alongside European Le Mans Series events.

details.name: Ultimate Prototype Cup
details.description:
The Prototype Endurance category combines the speed of prototype racing with the strategic challenges of endurance competition. Designed for teams looking to test their engineering and race management skills, this series demands both speed and durability to succeed over longer race distances.

With a mix of professional and amateur drivers, the class fosters teamwork and consistency. Managing fuel, tires, and driver changes is crucial, creating a dynamic contest where races are often decided in the final laps.

details.name: Ultimate Prototype Sprint Cup
details.description:
Prototype Sprint racing in the Ultimate Cup showcases lightweight, high-downforce prototypes competing in intense, short-distance battles. These purpose-built machines are designed for agility and speed, offering a thrilling spectacle as drivers fight for position on some of Europe’s most technical circuits.

With an emphasis on pure performance, the Prototype Sprint category highlights quick reflexes and precision driving. The close racing and rapid pace ensure exciting competition, making it a standout feature of the championship.

details.name: Ultimate Endurance Cup
details.description:
The Endurance GT Cup is a true test of consistency, strategy, and teamwork, featuring high-performance GT cars competing in long-distance races. Blending professional and amateur drivers, the category offers an exciting endurance racing experience where tactics and reliability play as much of a role as outright speed.

With a diverse grid featuring various GT machinery, the competition remains fierce from start to finish. Teams must balance speed with endurance, making strategic pit stops and managing tire wear to emerge victorious in this demanding format.

details.name: Ultimate Sprint Cup
details.description:
The Ultimate Cup Sprint Cup is a European sportscar championship. Race weekends consist of 4x 25 minute races.

The championship is open to several classes, including GT3, GT4 and single make classes such as Porsche Carrera Cup.

details.name: Prototype Winter Series
details.description:
The Prototype Winter Series is a premier sportscar racing series organized to provide competitive racing opportunities during the winter months. The series consists of endurance races, typically ranging from 1 to 3 hours, and takes place on some of the most renowned circuits in Southern Europe and other temperate locations.

The Prototype Winter Series runs two classes - LMP3 and Ligier JSP4. These cars are designed for both professional and amateur drivers, offering an excellent platform to maintain racing skills and develop new talent during the off-season.

details.name: Prototype Sports Winter Series
details.description: The Prototype Sports Winter Series (PTWS) is a newly expanded winter sportscar championship, designed to broaden competition beyond LMP3 machinery and welcome a wider variety of prototype-type sportscars. It runs during the January–March off-season, offering teams and drivers a high-quality platform to stay sharp, develop drivers, and race in temperate climates.

details.name: GT Winter Series
details.description:
The GT Winter Series is a sportscar series organized to provide competitive racing opportunities during the winter months. The series consists of sprint races, typically featuring multiple short races per weekend, and takes place on some of the most renowned circuits in Southern Europe and other temperate locations.

GT Winter series includes GT3, GT4 and single make series cars such as Porsche Cup. GT Winter series is part of the larger Winter Series package that runs through the winter in europe.

details.name: GT Summer Series
details.description: The GT Summer Series takes place from April to October on a selection of great international circuits in the surrounding Central- and Northern- European areas, offering one racing-weekend per month. It is an extension of the popular GT Winter Series, held in Spain and Portgual during the Northern Hemisphere winters

details.name: GT4 Winter Series
details.description:
The GT Winter Series is a sportscar series organized to provide competitive racing opportunities during the winter months. The series consists of sprint races, typically featuring multiple short races per weekend, and takes place on some of the most renowned circuits in Southern Europe and other temperate locations.

GT Winter series is dedicated to GT4 class cars and  is part of the larger Winter Series package that runs through the winter in europe.

details.name: Supercars
details.description:
The V8 Supercars Championship, now known as the Supercars Championship, is a premier touring car racing series in Australia, organized by Supercars Australia. The series consists of sprint and endurance races, ranging from 100 to 1000 kilometers, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits in Australia and New Zealand.

The Supercars Championship runs a single class featuring high-performance V8-powered touring cars from various manufacturers. The series is known for its intense, wheel-to-wheel racing and features a mix of professional drivers. With its unique blend of sprint and endurance events, including the prestigious Bathurst 1000, the championship ensures thrilling and competitive racing, showcasing the power and spectacle of V8 Supercars throughout the season.

details.name: Super2
details.description:
The Dunlop Super2 Series, also known as the Dunlop Series, is a second-tier touring car racing series in Australia, organized by Supercars Australia. The series consists of sprint races, typically featuring multiple short races per weekend, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits in Australia.

The Dunlop Super2 Series runs a single class featuring high-performance V8-powered touring cars, which are slightly older models compared to those used in the main Supercars Championship. The series is designed as a development category for emerging talent, providing young and aspiring drivers with a platform to hone their skills and gain experience in highly competitive racing conditions.

details.name: Super Utes
details.description:
The SuperUtes Series is a racing series in Australia, organized by Supercars Australia. The series consists of sprint races, typically featuring multiple short races per weekend, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits in Australia.

The SuperUtes Series runs a single class featuring production-based utility vehicles (utes) that have been heavily modified for racing.

details.name: Radical World Finals
details.description:
Radical Motorsport is one of the world’s most prolific race car manufacturers, with over 3,000 cars produced over nearly three decades. From its 1997 inception, Radical set out to create a customer-led niche in the world of motorsport, a race-bred thrill-a-minute experience on the racetrack. Over the years Radical has cemented it’s place in motorsport history, from setting Nürburgring records to competing at the prestigious 24 Hours of Le Mans.

A combination of lightweight construction, superbike-derived technology and aerodynamic downforce delivers performance faster than a supercar with a driver-focused and visceral driving experience. Now in its third decade, the Radical stable includes the entry-level SR1 XXR, the best-selling SR3 XXR alongside the turbo-charged SR10 XXR, and the iconic RXC. All models are solely designed, engineered and built at Radical’s Cambridgeshire headquarters in England. A global dealer network spanning over 20 countries continues to grow, and there are now multiple single-make Radical Cup championships across four different continents, including the Radical Cup North America which races alongside several IndyCar weekends, the long-standing Radical Cup UK, and new for 2026 the Radical Cup Europe supporting the popular Creventic 24H Series.

Following the success of the previous Radical World Finals events in Las Vegas (2022), Portimão (2023), and Abu Dhabi (2024) the 2025 Radical World Finals will return to North America at Virginia International Raceway. Located in Alton, Virginia, Virginia International Raceway (VIR) is renowned for its challenging layout and picturesque setting. The 3.27-mile road course features high-speed straights, technical corners, and dramatic elevation changes, making it a favourite among drivers and spectators alike.

The Radical World Finals 2025 will feature four classes of competition. Drivers piloting the brand’s SR10 and SR3 race cars will vie for World Championship titles in their respective divisions, plus a new Gold class for drivers over the age of 30 competing in the competitive SR3 1500 class.

details.name: Porsche Supercup
details.description:
The Porsche Supercup is a premier international one-make racing series organized by Porsche AG. The series consists of sprint races, typically lasting around 30 minutes each, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits around the world as a support series to Formula 1 events.

The Porsche Supercup runs a single class featuring identical Porsche 911 GT3 Cup cars and acts as the top rung of the Porsche Cup ladder for Porsche Carrera Cup national champions to move to.

details.name: British F4
details.description:
The British F4 Championship is a junior single-seater racing series in the United Kingdom, organized by MotorSport Vision (MSV). The series consists of sprint races, typically featuring three 20-minute races per weekend, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits in the UK.

The British F4 Championship runs a single class featuring Formula 4 spec cars. These cars are designed as an entry-level category for young drivers, typically aged 15 to 18, aiming to progress through the ranks of motorsport. The series is part of the TOCA (BTCC) support package

details.name: Stock Car Pro
details.description:
The Brazilian Stock Car Pro Series, established in 1979, is Brazil’s premier touring car racing championship. It features modified, production-based cars from manufacturers like Chevrolet and Toyota. The series operates under regulations designed to ensure parity, with cars constructed to similar specifications to promote close racing. The season consists of multiple rounds held on various Brazilian circuits, including iconic tracks like Interlagos and Curitiba, with each event featuring two races to maximize competition. The series is managed by Vicar, an organization responsible for Brazil’s top motorsport categories.

Drivers competing in the Stock Car Pro Series are primarily Brazilian, although it attracts a few international competitors. Races are streamed free, with no geoblocking, on YouTube.

details.name: Stock Series
details.description:
The Brazilian Stock Car Pro Series, established in 1979, is Brazil’s premier touring car racing championship. It features modified, production-based cars from manufacturers like Chevrolet and Toyota. The series operates under regulations designed to ensure parity, with cars constructed to similar specifications to promote close racing. Drivers competing in the Stock Car Pro Series are primarily Brazilian, although it attracts a few international competitors. Races are streamed free, with no geoblocking, on YouTube.

details.name: TCR World Tour
details.description:
The TCR World Tour is a premier international touring car racing series organized by WSC Group. The series consists of sprint races, typically featuring multiple races per weekend, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits around the world.

Like IGTC, TCR World Tour has a unique calendar setup, with some races being stand-alone events, and others being part of various regional championships. This ensures a diverse and unique grid at each event.

details.name: TCR Europe
details.description:
The TCR Europe Series is a touring car racing championship in Europe, organized by the WSC Group. The series consists of sprint races, typically featuring multiple races per weekend, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits across Europe.

The TCR Europe Series runs a single class featuring TCR spec cars from various manufacturers. These touring cars are based on production models, providing a cost-effective yet highly competitive platform for racing.

details.name: TCR Europe Cup
details.description:
The TCR Europe Cup is a stepping-stone between the National TCR championships and the
international TCR categories. The Cup aims to introduce a fresh sporting format that allows two
drivers to share a car during the event, each benefiting from dedicated track time and individual
exposure.

details.name: TCR South America
details.description:
The TCR South America Series is a touring car racing championship held across Argentina, Brazil, Uruguay, and Paraguay, run under the global TCR regulations. The series features sprint races, usually with multiple races per weekend, and competes at some of the most iconic circuits in the region.

TCR South America runs a single class of competition featuring TCR-spec cars from a range of manufacturers. These touring cars are based on production models, providing a cost-effective yet highly competitive platform for racing. When racing in Brazil, the series often supports the prestigious Stock Car Pro Series.

details.name: TCR Italy
details.description:
The TCR Italy Touring Car Championship is one of the premier touring car series in Italy, sanctioned by the Italian motorsport authority ACI Sport. The championship features a calendar of sprint races held at renowned circuits across Italy, including iconic venues such as Monza, Imola, and Mugello.

TCR Italy runs a single class of competition using TCR specification touring cars from a variety of manufacturers. Based on production models, these cars offer a cost-effective entry point into top-level touring car racing, while delivering close, competitive action that showcases both driver talent and engineering performance.

details.name: TCR UK
details.description:
The TCR UK Championship is a touring car racing series in the United Kingdom, organized by the WSC Group. The series consists of sprint races, typically featuring multiple races per weekend, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits across the UK.

The TCR UK Championship runs a single class featuring TCR spec cars from various manufacturers. These touring cars are based on production models, providing a cost-effective yet highly competitive platform for racing.

details.name: TC America
details.description:
The TC America Series is a touring car racing championship in North America, organized by the SRO Motorsports Group. The series consists of sprint races, typically featuring multiple races per weekend, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits across the United States and Canada.

The TC America Series runs multiple classes, including TC, TCA, and TCX, accommodating a wide range of touring cars from various manufacturers. These classes feature production-based models with varying levels of performance and modifications, providing a highly competitive and diverse racing environment.

details.name: TCR Eastern Europe
details.description:
The TCR Eastern Europe Series is a touring car racing championship held across Central and Eastern Europe, organized under the TCR global regulations. The series features sprint races, usually with multiple races per weekend, and visits some of the region’s most renowned circuits.

TCR Eastern Europe runs a single class of TCR-spec cars from a variety of manufacturers. These cars are based on production models, offering a cost-effective yet fiercely competitive platform for drivers and teams alike.

details.name: Italian F4
details.description:
The Italian F4 Championship is a junior single-seater racing series in Italy, organized by ACI Sport. The series consists of sprint races, typically featuring multiple races per weekend, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits across Italy.

The Italian F4 Championship runs a single class featuring Formula 4 spec cars. These cars are designed as an entry-level category for young drivers, typically aged 15 to 18, aiming to progress through the ranks of motorsport.

details.name: Japanese F4
details.description:
he Japanese F4 Championship is a premier junior single-seater racing series in Japan, organized by the Japanese Automobile Federation (JAF) and supported by the FIA. The series consists of sprint races, typically featuring multiple races per weekend, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits across Japan.

The Japanese F4 Championship runs a single class featuring Formula 4 spec cars. These cars are designed as an entry-level category for young drivers, typically aged 15 to 18, aiming to progress through the ranks of motorsport.

The Australian F4 Championship runs a single class featuring Formula 4 spec cars. These cars are designed as an entry-level category for young drivers, typically aged 15 to 18, aiming to progress through the ranks of motorsport.

details.name: F4 US
details.description:
The F4 U.S. Championship is a premier junior single-seater racing series in the United States, organized by SCCA Pro Racing and sanctioned by the FIA. The series consists of sprint races, typically featuring multiple races per weekend, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits across North America.

The F4 U.S. Championship runs a single class featuring Formula 4 spec cars. These cars are designed as an entry-level category for young drivers, typically aged 15 to 18, aiming to progress through the ranks of motorsport.

details.name: JS F4
details.description: The Ligier JS F4 Series is an entry-level, single-seater racing championship that debuted in 2024. Part of the SpeedTour events, the series serves as a developmental platform for drivers transitioning from karting to open-wheel racing, providing a structured environment to hone their skills before advancing to higher tiers like the Formula 4 United States Championship (F4 U.S.).

details.name: NACAM F4
details.description:
The NACAM Formula 4 Championship is a premier junior single-seater racing series in the NACAM region, which includes North America, Central America, and parts of the Caribbean. Organized by FIA NACAM and sanctioned by the FIA, the series consists of sprint races, typically featuring multiple races per weekend, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits across the NACAM region.

The NACAM Formula 4 Championship runs a single class featuring Formula 4 spec cars. These cars are designed as an entry-level category for young drivers, typically aged 15 to 18, aiming to progress through the ranks of motorsport.

details.name: UAE4
details.description:
UAE4 is a regional motorsport championship designed to develop the next generation of racing talent. Governed by FIA Formula 4 regulations, it provides young drivers with a competitive platform to gain experience and showcase their skills in state-of-the-art single-seater cars. The series features high-quality races across iconic circuits in the Middle East, including venues in the UAE and Bahrain.

As a stepping stone to higher levels of motorsport, UAE4 emphasizes driver development through intense racing and professional guidance. The championship is known for its accessibility and international appeal, attracting drivers from around the world aiming to progress through the motorsport ladder.

details.name: F4 Saudi Arabia
details.description:
F4 Saudi Arabia is a regional motorsport championship created to nurture the next generation of racing talent. Governed by FIA Formula 4 regulations, it offers young drivers a highly competitive environment to gain experience and showcase their abilities in state-of-the-art single-seater cars. The series hosts thrilling races at premier circuits across Saudi Arabia, highlighting the nation’s growing role in international motorsport.

As a key step on the path to higher categories, F4 Saudi Arabia places strong emphasis on driver development through close racing and professional guidance. The championship is designed for accessibility and global appeal, attracting rising talents from around the world who are eager to climb the motorsport ladder.

details.name: Formula Trophy
details.description:
Formula Trophy is a regional Formula 4 level event. It serves as an additional competitive platform for junior single-seater drivers, typically held as a support event to major motorsport series within the United Arab Emirates. The competition uses homologated Formula 4 cars and follows standard technical and sporting regulations aligned with international Formula 4 requirements.

The event offers drivers the opportunity to gain race mileage, operate within professional team environments, and accumulate experience ahead of full-season championships. Participation is open to eligible drivers meeting FIA licensing criteria, and the format generally includes practice sessions, qualifying, and multiple races. The Formula Trophy functions as part of the progression system from karting to higher-level single-seater categories

details.name: F4 Brazil
details.description:
The Brazilian Stock Car Pro Series, established in 1979, is Brazil’s premier touring car racing championship. It features modified, production-based cars from manufacturers like Chevrolet and Toyota. The series operates under regulations designed to ensure parity, with cars constructed to similar specifications to promote close racing. The season consists of multiple rounds held on various Brazilian circuits, including iconic tracks like Interlagos and Curitiba, with each event featuring two races to maximize competition. The series is managed by Vicar, an organization responsible for Brazil’s top motorsport categories.

Drivers competing in the Stock Car Pro Series are primarily Brazilian, although it attracts a few international competitors. Races are streamed free, with no geoblocking, on YouTube.

details.name: USF Pro 2000
details.description:
The USF Pro 2000 Championship Presented by Continental Tire is an American open-wheel racing series that serves as the third step on the USF Pro Championships ladder, positioned between the USF2000 Championship and INDY NXT. Sanctioned by the United States Auto Club (USAC) and operated by Andersen Promotions, the series provides a platform for drivers aiming to progress to higher tiers of motorsport, particularly the NTT INDYCAR SERIES.

Competitors in the USF Pro 2000 Championship drive Tatuus IP-22 chassis, powered by Mazda engines, with the series transitioning to Continental Tire as the official tire supplier. The champion of the series is awarded a scholarship to advance to INDY NXT, facilitating progression within the USF Pro Championships ladder.

details.name: USF 2000
details.description:
The USF2000 Championship Presented by Continental Tire is an American open-wheel racing series that serves as the second step on the USF Pro Championships ladder, positioned between USF Juniors and USF Pro 2000. Sanctioned by the United States Auto Club (USAC) and operated by Andersen Promotions, the series provides a platform for drivers aiming to progress to higher tiers of motorsport, particularly the NTT INDYCAR SERIES.

Competitors in the USF2000 Championship drive Tatuus USF-22 chassis, powered by Mazda 2.0-liter MZR engines, with Continental Tire as the official tire supplier. The champion of the series is awarded a scholarship to advance to the USF Pro 2000 Championship, facilitating progression within the USF Pro Championships ladder.

details.name: USF Junior
details.description:
The USF Juniors Championship Presented by Continental Tire is an American open-wheel racing series that serves as the entry-level step on the USF Pro Championships ladder, which provides a scholarship-funded path to the NTT INDYCAR SERIES and the Indianapolis 500. 

Inaugurated in 2022, USF Juniors offers a platform for drivers transitioning from karting or other entry-level racing categories, providing valuable experience on a variety of circuits, including road courses and street circuits.

The Porsche Carrera Cup Asia runs a single class featuring identical Porsche 911 GT3 Cup cars.

Established as the first Carrera Cup series outside of Europe, it has become a cornerstone of Brazilian motorsport, delivering highly competitive sprint races typically lasting around 25–30 minutes. The series visits some of the country’s most renowned circuits, showcasing close, high-intensity racing throughout the season.

The Porsche Carrera Cup Brazil features a single-make grid of identical Porsche 911 GT3 Cup cars, ensuring a level playing field where driver skill and consistency are the key differentiators.

details.name: Porsche Sprint Challenge GB
details.description:
The Porsche Sprint Challenge GB is a competitive one-make racing series in the United Kingdom, organized by Porsche Motorsport GB and BARC. The series features sprint-style races, typically lasting around 30 minutes, and supports the British Touring Car Championship.

The Porsche Sprint Challenge GB features Porsche 718 Cayman GT4 Clubsport. Drivers compete within their respective classes while sharing the same track, creating multi-class sprint racing that highlights both professional and emerging drivers within Porsche’s global motorsport ladder system.

details.name: Porsche Sprint Challenge NA
details.description:
The Porsche Sprint Challenge North America (NA) is a competitive one-make racing series in North America, organized by Porsche Motorsport North America. The series features sprint-style races, typically lasting around 30–40 minutes, and runs on major circuits across the United States and Canada, often alongside other Porsche and IMSA events.

The Porsche Sprint Challenge North America features multiple classes based on Porsche customer race cars, including the Porsche 911 GT3 Cup (992 generation) and the Porsche 718 Cayman GT4 Clubsport. Drivers compete within their respective classes while sharing the same track, creating multi-class sprint racing that highlights both professional and emerging drivers within Porsche’s global motorsport ladder system.

details.name: Porsche Sprint Challenge USA West
details.description:
The Porsche Sprint Challenge USA West is a regional one-make racing series in the United States, organized by Porsche Motorsport North America. The championship features sprint-style races, typically lasting around 30–40 minutes, and takes place at well-known circuits across the western United States.

The Porsche Sprint Challenge USA West includes multiple classes based on Porsche customer race cars, such as the Porsche 911 GT3 Cup and the Porsche 718 Cayman GT4 Clubsport. Drivers compete within their respective classes while sharing the same track, providing competitive multi-class sprint racing and serving as an entry point into Porsche’s global motorsport development ladder.

details.name: Porsche Endurance Challenge NA
details.description:
The Porsche Endurance Challenge North America (NA) is a multi-class endurance racing series in the United States, organized by Porsche Motorsport North America. The championship features longer endurance-style races, typically lasting 90 minutes, and takes place at major road courses across the United States.

The Porsche Endurance Challenge North America includes multiple classes based on Porsche customer race cars, including the Porsche 911 GT3 Cup and the Porsche 718 Cayman GT4 Clubsport. Drivers compete in either solo or multi-driver lineups, creating strategic endurance racing within Porsche’s customer racing ecosystem and providing additional track time for teams competing in Porsche’s North American sprint series.

details.name: Porsche Cup Middle East
details.description:
The Porsche Carrera Cup Middle East is a premier one-make racing series featuring identical Porsche 911 GT3 Cup cars, providing a platform for both local and international drivers across Amateur, Pro-Am, and Professional categories to compete on equal footing.

Established in 2009 as the Porsche GT3 Cup Challenge Middle East, the series was renamed the Porsche Sprint Challenge Middle East in 2019, and in 2023, it evolved into the Porsche Carrera Cup Middle East, aligning with other prestigious Porsche Carrera Cup championships worldwide.

details.name: 992 Endurance Cup
details.description: Porsche Motorsport and series organiser Creventic have created a new event for the current 911 GT3 Cup: the Michelin 992 Endurance Cup powered by Porsche Motorsport. The 12-hour race will be run on the 6/7 September 2024 at the Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps in Belgium. This event is reserved exclusively for customer teams fielding 992-generation 911 GT3 Cup cars.

details.name: Ferrari Challenge NA
details.description:
The Ferrari Challenge North America is a prestigious single-make racing series organized by Ferrari. The series consists of multiple races held across North America, featuring Ferrari 488 Challenge Evo cars. These races are typically sprint races, ranging from 30 to 40 minutes, and showcase the skills of both amateur and professional drivers.

Currently, the series runs two main classes. The Trofeo Pirelli class, which features experienced and professional drivers, and the Coppa Shell class, which consists of less experienced, amateur drivers. Both classes provide an exciting platform for Ferrari enthusiasts to compete in a highly competitive and professional racing environment.

details.name: Ferrari Challenge Europe
details.description:
The Ferrari Challenge Europe is a prestigious racing series organized by Ferrari that features a series of sprint races across various European circuits. The series is exclusively for Ferrari drivers and cars, emphasizing competitive racing with a focus on Ferrari's performance models.

The Ferrari Challenge Europe typically consists of two main categories: the Trofeo Pirelli and the Coppa Shell. The Trofeo Pirelli category is for more experienced drivers and often features the latest Ferrari models in a highly competitive environment. The Coppa Shell category, on the other hand, caters to less experienced drivers and those newer to the Ferrari Challenge series, providing a more accessible entry point while still maintaining high standards of racing.

details.name: Ferrari Challenge UK
details.description: The Ferrari Challenge UK is a prestigious racing series organized by Ferrari, featuring sprint races at top British circuits. It includes two main categories: Trofeo Pirelli for more experienced drivers with the latest Ferrari models, and Coppa Shell for less experienced drivers. The series follows a Pro-Am driver setup, offering a competitive yet accessible racing experience with shorter, high-intensity races.

details.name: Ferrari Challenge Japan
details.description: The Ferrari Challenge Japan is a premier racing series organized by Ferrari, showcasing sprint races across leading Japanese circuits. The series features two main categories: Trofeo Pirelli for experienced drivers with the latest Ferrari models, and Coppa Shell for those with less racing experience. Following a Pro-Am driver setup, it offers an exciting blend of competitive racing and Ferrari's performance excellence in a high-intensity, accessible format.

details.name: Lamborghini World Finals
details.description:
The Lamborghini Super Trofeo World Finals is the culminating event of Lamborghini’s global single-make racing program, bringing together competitors from the Europe, North America, and Asia championships. All participants race in identical Lamborghini Huracán Super Trofeo EVO2 cars — purpose-built machines powered by a 5.2-liter naturally aspirated V10 engine, featuring rear-wheel drive, a sequential six-speed gearbox, and advanced aerodynamic design for maximum performance.

The event features multiple classes — Pro, Pro-Am, Am, and Lamborghini Cup — ensuring close competition among drivers of all experience levels. Across the weekend, entrants compete in two 50-minute races with mandatory pit stops, combining raw speed with strategic depth.

Held at iconic circuits such as Vallelunga, Misano, and Jerez, the World Finals crown the overall Lamborghini Super Trofeo World Champions, uniting the best teams and drivers from every continental series in one of the most prestigious events on the GT racing calendar.

details.name: Lamborghini Super Trofeo NA
details.description:
The Lamborghini Super Trofeo North America is a single-make racing series featuring the Lamborghini Huracán Super Trofeo EVO2, a purpose-built race car powered by a 5.2-liter naturally aspirated V10 engine. The series is part of the IMSA calendar, running as a support series alongside IMSA WeatherTech SportsCar Championship events. The cars are equipped with rear-wheel drive, a sequential six-speed gearbox, and aerodynamic upgrades designed for high-speed competition.

The series features multiple classes, including Pro, Pro-Am, Am, and Lamborghini Cup, accommodating drivers of varying experience levels. Each race weekend includes two 50-minute races, with mandatory pit stops adding a strategic element to the competition. Teams and drivers compete on some of the most renowned circuits in North America, including Daytona International Speedway, WeatherTech Raceway Laguna Seca, and Road America, as part of the IMSA paddock.

details.name: Lamborghini Super Trofeo EU
details.description:
The Lamborghini Super Trofeo Europe is a single-make racing series featuring the Lamborghini Huracán Super Trofeo EVO2, a purpose-built race car powered by a 5.2-liter naturally aspirated V10 engine. The series is part of the SRO calendar, running as a support series alongside IGT World Challenge events. The cars are equipped with rear-wheel drive, a sequential six-speed gearbox, and aerodynamic upgrades designed for high-speed competition.

The series features multiple classes, including Pro, Pro-Am, Am, and Lamborghini Cup, accommodating drivers of varying experience levels. Each race weekend includes two 50-minute races, with mandatory pit stops adding a strategic element to the competition.

details.name: Lamborghini Super Trofeo Asia
details.description: The Lamborghini Super Trofeo Asia is a premier one-make racing series organized by Lamborghini Squadra Corse, featuring identical Lamborghini Huracán Super Trofeo Evo2 cars. Established in 2012, the series provides a competitive platform for both professional and amateur drivers across the Asia-Pacific region.

details.name: Mustang Challenge
details.description: The IMSA Mustang Challenge is a North American single-make racing series that debuted in 2024, featuring the Ford Mustang Dark Horse R race cars. Sanctioned by the International Motor Sports Association (IMSA), the series aims to provide a competitive platform for drivers and teams, fostering progression within Ford’s racing ladder.

details.name: Toyota GR Cup NA
details.description:
The Toyota GR Cup North America is a single-make racing series launched by Toyota Gazoo Racing North America (TGRNA) in 2023. This championship features drivers competing exclusively in Toyota GR86 vehicles, which are production models modified for racing while retaining their stock engines. 

The series aims to celebrate car culture and motorsports by providing an accessible platform for enthusiasts to engage in competitive racing.

details.name: Mazda MX-5 Cup
details.description:
The IMSA WeatherTech SportsCar Championship is a premier North American sportscar racing series organized by the International Motor Sports Association (IMSA). The series features endurance races, ranging from 2.4 hours to 24 hours, and includes the prestigious Rolex 24 at Daytona. Currently, the series runs multiple classes, with the GTP (Grand Touring Prototype) as the top tier, featuring manufacturer-produced hybrid prototypes. The LMP2 (Le Mans Prototype 2) class caters to privateer teams using spec chassis and engines, while the LMP3 (Le Mans Prototype 3) serves as an entry-level prototype class aimed at developing drivers and teams.

In addition to the prototype classes, the GT Daytona Pro (GTD Pro) class consists of GT3-spec cars with professional drivers, and the GT Daytona (GTD) class follows a similar format but with a Pro-Am driver setup, combining professional and amateur drivers. These classes ensure a diverse and competitive field, showcasing both cutting-edge technology and driver skill across a range of world-class circuits.

details.name: McLaren Trophy EU
details.description:
The McLaren Trophy Europe is a single-make racing championship established by McLaren in 2023. It is designed to challenge Bronze and Silver drivers in 50-minute races with pit stops for driver changes. The championship supports the Fanatec GT World Challenge Europe powered by AWS, featuring 10 rounds spread over five events at renowned circuits across Europe.
  
For the 2025 season, the McLaren Trophy Europe will introduce the McLaren Artura Trophy Evo, an upgraded race car boasting 585PS, revised aerodynamics, wider tyres, and new push-to-pass technology capable of temporarily boosting power output to 620PS.

details.name: McLaren Trophy US
details.description:
The McLaren Trophy US is a single-make racing championship established by McLaren in 2025. It is designed to challenge Bronze and Silver drivers in 50-minute races with pit stops for driver changes. The championship supports the Fanatec GT World Challenge America Series, featuring 10 rounds spread over five events at renowned circuits across the US.
  
For the 2025 season, the McLaren Trophy US will introduce the McLaren Artura Trophy Evo, an upgraded race car boasting 585PS, revised aerodynamics, wider tyres, and new push-to-pass technology capable of temporarily boosting power output to 620PS.

details.name: International GT
