details.description: International GT (IGT) is a North American racing series that features three distinct championships for various GT cars. Primarily catering to sportsman drivers, with occasional participation from professional driver-coaches in endurance races, IGT organizes events where all three divisions compete simultaneously on the same track. Each class contends for individual honors within their exclusive IGT run group. A typical race weekend comprises at least one endurance race and two sprint races that contribute to the season-long championships.

details.name: Prototype Sprint Series
details.description:
The Prototype Sprint Series Association (PSSA), presented by Hankook, is a motorsports series established in 2023 by Parella Motorsports Holdings (PMH). It features prototype race cars competing in sprint race formats at iconic road courses across the United States. 

The series includes various prototype models, such as Radical, Ligier, Revolution, and Wolf, all competing on Hankook racing tires. Drivers are required to hold an SVRA Competition License or SVRA Super License, depending on their class. Each race weekend typically consists of five on-track sessions, including two feature races.

details.name: Trans Am National Series
details.description:
The Trans Am national series is the flagship championship of the Trans Am Series—a long‐standing American road racing series that has been running since 1966. Originally launched as the Trans‑American Sedan Championship, the series evolved over the decades into a premier professional racing series that features production-based or “silhouette” race cars. 

Today, competitors race in multiple classes—such as TA (the traditional high-performance “muscle car” class), TA2 (a class featuring more cost-controlled, competitive cars), and additional production-based classes like XGT, SGT, and GT—across a schedule of events held at some of North America’s most iconic road courses and street circuits

details.name: Trans Am West Series
details.description: The Trans Am West series—also known as the West Coast Championship—is the regional offshoot of the historic Trans Am Series. Launched in 2017, it features the same multi-class, production-based racing seen nationwide but with a schedule tailored to iconic West Coast tracks like Sonoma Raceway and Buttonwillow. It offers local teams and drivers their own championship while sharing the legacy of the overall Trans Am Series.

details.name: NASCAR Cup
details.description:
The NASCAR Cup Series is the premier stock car racing series in the United States, organized by the National Association for Stock Car Auto Racing (NASCAR). The series consists of a mix of oval and road course races, typically featuring 30+ points-paying races over a season, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits across North America.

The NASCAR Cup Series features highly modified, high-performance stock cars from manufacturers such as Chevrolet, Ford, and Toyota.

details.name: NASCAR O'Reilly
details.description:
The NASCAR Xfinity Series is a premier stock car racing series in the United States, organized by the National Association for Stock Car Auto Racing (NASCAR). The series consists of a mix of oval and road course races, typically featuring 33 points-paying races over a season, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits across North America.

The series serves as a proving ground for up-and-coming drivers looking to make their mark and progress to the NASCAR Cup Series, while also featuring experienced drivers and Cup Series veterans.

details.name: NASCAR Trucks
details.description:
The NASCAR Craftsman Truck Series is a premier stock car racing series in the United States, organized by the National Association for Stock Car Auto Racing (NASCAR). The series consists of a mix of oval and road course races, typically featuring 23 points-paying races over a season, and takes place on some of the most iconic circuits across North America.

The NASCAR Craftsman Truck Series features high-performance pickup trucks from manufacturers such as Chevrolet, Ford, and Toyota.

details.name: NASCAR Modified Tour
details.description: The NASCAR Modified Tour is a regional racing series featuring highly customized stock cars with open front wheels and distinctive, low-slung bodies. Focused primarily in the northeastern United States, the series races on short tracks ranging from 0.25 to 1 mile, emphasizing close, competitive racing. The cars are powered by small-block V8 engines. Races are held at various historic and local tracks, making the series accessible to grassroots racers.

details.name: NASCAR Canada
details.description: The NASCAR Canada is the premier stock car racing series in Canada. It features full-bodied, V8-powered cars that closely resemble those used in the NASCAR Xfinity Series. The series competes on a mix of oval tracks, road courses, and street circuits across Canada

details.name: NASCAR Mexico
details.description: The NASCAR Mexico Series is the top stock car racing series in Mexico, featuring cars similar to those used in the NASCAR Xfinity Series but adapted for local competition. The series races on a variety of circuits, including oval tracks, road courses, and short tracks across Mexico, offering diverse challenges to drivers and teams. The cars are powered by V8 engines and are known for their close, competitive racing, making the series a central part of Mexico’s motorsport scene.

details.name: NASCAR Brazil
details.description:
The NASCAR Brasil Series is the premier stock car racing championship in Brazil, featuring purpose-built race cars inspired by NASCAR machinery and adapted for the Brazilian market. The series competes on a mix of circuits across the country, including permanent road courses, street circuits, and select oval-style layouts, providing a wide range of technical and driving challenges.

Cars are powered by V8 engines and use a controlled technical package to ensure close competition, placing emphasis on driver skill and racecraft. Known for its competitive fields and growing fanbase, the NASCAR Brasil Series plays a key role in Brazil’s national motorsport ladder and in expanding NASCAR’s presence in South America.

details.name: EuroNASCAR
details.description: The NASCAR Whelen Euro Series is the premier stock car racing series in Europe, featuring cars similar to those used in the NASCAR Xfinity Series, but tailored for European competition. The series races on a mix of circuits, including road courses, oval tracks, and street circuits across various European countries, presenting diverse challenges for both drivers and teams. Powered by V8 engines, the cars are designed for close, competitive racing, establishing the series as a key part of Europe’s stock car racing landscape.

details.name: ARCA
details.description: The ARCA Menards Series is a premier stock car racing series in the United States, organized by the Automobile Racing Club of America (ARCA). The series consists of a diverse schedule of oval tracks, road courses, and dirt tracks, typically featuring around 20 points-paying races throughout the season. ARCA places itself as a feeder series to NASCAR and regularly joins them as part of their weekend schedules.

details.name: ARCA Series West
details.description: The ARCA Series West is a regional stock car racing series in the United States, organized by the Automobile Racing Club of America (ARCA).

details.name: ARCA Series East
details.description: The ARCA Series East is a regional stock car racing series in the United States, organized by the Automobile Racing Club of America (ARCA).

details.name: CARS Tour
details.description: The ARCA Series East is a regional stock car racing series in the United States, organized by the Automobile Racing Club of America (ARCA).

details.name: World of Outlaws Sprint
details.description:
The World of Outlaws Sprint Car Series is the pinnacle of winged sprint car racing in North America, renowned for its relentless pace, fierce competition, and electric atmosphere. The series features the best dirt track drivers in the world, competing in 900-horsepower, methanol-fueled sprint cars on high-speed ovals across the United States.

These lightweight, high-downforce machines are built for explosive acceleration and razor-sharp handling, making every lap a spectacle of precision and bravery. With a demanding coast-to-coast schedule that includes marquee events like the Knoxville Nationals and the Kings Royal, the series pushes drivers and teams to their absolute limits.

Known as “The Greatest Show on Dirt,” the World of Outlaws Sprint Car Series delivers heart-pounding action and a passionate fan experience, solidifying its status as a legendary force in American motorsport.

details.name: World of Outlaws LMs
details.description:
The World of Outlaws Late Model Series is America’s premier dirt late model championship, showcasing high-powered racing across the country’s most iconic short tracks. It provides a thrilling platform for both seasoned veterans and rising stars to compete at the highest level of dirt late model racing.

Drivers pilot custom-built, purpose-engineered machines boasting over 800 horsepower, battling on dirt ovals from Florida to North Dakota in a high-intensity calendar packed with prestigious events and major purses. Each car is finely tuned for performance and durability, making the series a true test of driver skill and mechanical expertise.

With a passionate fanbase, intense rivalries, and a legacy of producing dirt racing legends, the World of Outlaws Late Model Series is a cornerstone of American motorsport, delivering raw, unfiltered racing excitement year after year.

details.name: USAC Silver Crown
details.description:
The USAC Silver Crown Championship sits at the top of the USAC pyramid, combining high speeds with technical versatility across both dirt and paved ovals. The cars are larger and heavier than Sprint Cars and Midgets, demanding a different driving style and a broader skill set.

Often featuring longer races and historic venues, Silver Crown is considered the pinnacle of USAC competition. It attracts experienced drivers and serves as the ultimate test within the organisation’s national series structure.

details.name: USAC Sprint Car
details.description:
The USAC Sprint Car Championship is one of the most competitive dirt racing series in North America, featuring powerful, non-wing sprint cars that demand precision and car control. Raced primarily on short dirt ovals, the series delivers intense, wheel-to-wheel action and is widely regarded as a proving ground for top-tier oval racers.

With a deep talent pool and a long-standing heritage, the championship has produced many of the sport’s leading drivers. Success in Sprint Cars often marks a driver as ready to compete at the highest levels of American oval racing.

details.name: USAC Midget
details.description:
The USAC Midget National Championship represents the foundation of USAC’s premier ladder, showcasing lightweight, high-powered cars on tight dirt ovals. Known for close racing and high entry counts, Midget competition places a strong emphasis on racecraft, adaptability, and driver development.

The series has a rich history of nurturing future stars, with many drivers progressing through the ranks into Sprint Cars and beyond. It remains a key starting point for those looking to build a career in US oval racing.

details.name: Lucas Oil Late Models
details.description:
The Lucas Oil Late Model Dirt Series is a premier dirt late model touring series in the United States, owned and operated by Lucas Oil. Established in 2005, the series features high-powered, purpose-built chassis equipped with aluminum-head V8 engines producing over 800 horsepower. 

The series competes on dirt ovals across the country, primarily throughout the East Coast and Midwest.

details.name: High Limit Racing
details.description:
High Limit Racing is a fast-rising force in American sprint car racing, offering a premier national platform that blends grassroots passion with top-tier competition. Founded by NASCAR star Kyle Larson and industry veteran Brad Sweet, the series is designed to elevate sprint car racing with bigger purses, marquee events, and expanded exposure for drivers and fans alike.

Drivers compete in powerful 410ci winged sprint cars—lightweight machines delivering over 900 horsepower—on dirt ovals across the country.

details.name: WRC
details.description:
The FIA World Rally Championship (WRC) is an international rally series organized by the Fédération Internationale de l’Automobile (FIA). The series consists of challenging rallies held on various terrains, ranging from gravel and tarmac to snow and ice, and includes iconic events such as the Rallye Monte-Carlo and Safari Rally..

Currently, the series runs 2 main classes. The Rally1 class features manufacturer-backed cars, while the WRC2 class consists of Rally2 spec cars and includes a mix of professional and semi-professional drivers.

details.name: ERX
details.description:
The FIA European Rallycross Championship (ERX) is a rallycross series organized by the Fédération Internationale de l’Automobile (FIA). The series features short, high-intensity races on mixed surfaces, combining elements of rally and circuit racing, and includes prestigious events.

Currently, the series runs multiple classes, with the top-tier RX1e class featuring electric rallycross cars driven by professional drivers. Another prominent class is RX2e, which serves as a platform for emerging talent and future stars in rallycross, also using electric vehicles to promote sustainable motorsport.

details.name: ERC
details.description:
The FIA European Rally Championship (ERC) is an international rally series organized by the Fédération Internationale de l’Automobile (FIA). The series consists of rallies held across Europe on various terrains, ranging from gravel and tarmac to snow and ice, and includes prestigious events such as the Rally Islas Canarias.

Currently, the series runs multiple classes, including the top-tier ERC1 class, which features Rally2 spec cars driven by professional and semi-professional drivers, and the ERC3 class, which includes Rally3 cars.

details.name: ARA National
details.description:
The American Rally Association (ARA) is the premier stage rally championship in the United States, sanctioned by the United States Auto Club (USAC). The series spans a diverse range of events held across the country, challenging drivers with terrains that include gravel roads, forest tracks, snow-covered trails, and high-speed tarmac sections.

The championship features multiple classes, with the top-tier Open 4WD class showcasing high-performance rally cars driven by some of the most experienced teams in North America. Supporting classes such as Limited 4WD, Open 2WD, and Limited 2WD provide opportunities for emerging talent and privateer teams, making the ARA a competitive and inclusive platform for rallying at all levels. Notable events on the calendar include the Ojibwe Forests Rally, Oregon Trail Rally, and the iconic Sno*Drift Rally.

details.name: Canadian Rally Champ
details.description:
The Canadian Rally Championship (CRC) is the premier national stage rally championship in Canada, sanctioned by RallySport Canada (RSC). The series spans a wide variety of events across the country, from the forests of Québec and Ontario to the rugged landscapes of British Columbia, challenging crews on surfaces that include gravel, snow, ice, and mixed-surface tarmac.

The championship features multiple competition classes, with the headline Open 4WD category showcasing highly developed turbocharged rally cars driven by Canada’s top rally teams. Supporting classes such as Open 2WD, Limited 4WD, and Production create a clear ladder for developing drivers and privateer competitors, making the CRC both fiercely competitive and highly accessible. Signature rounds on the calendar include Rallye Baie-des-Chaleurs, Rocky Mountain Rally, and the legendary Rallye Perce-Neige, one of North America’s most famous winter rallies.

details.name: Extreme E
details.description:
The Extreme E Championship is an international off-road racing series organized by Extreme E Holdings. The series features electric SUVs competing in remote locations around the world, emphasizing sustainability and raising awareness about climate change. The races take place in diverse and challenging environments, such as deserts, glaciers, rainforests, and coastal areas.

Currently, the series runs a single class with identical electric SUVs, the Odyssey 21, driven by mixed-gender teams of professional drivers. The format includes short, high-intensity races with a focus on promoting environmental responsibility and innovation in electric vehicle technology.

details.name: MotoGP
details.description:
The MotoGP World Championship is an international motorcycle racing series organized by the Fédération Internationale de Motocyclisme (FIM). The series consists of multiple Grand Prix events held on renowned circuits worldwide, including locations such as Italy, Japan, and Spain.

MotoGP has two races per weekend, with a Sprint race taking place on Saturday before the main race on Sunday.

details.name: Moto2
details.description:
The Moto2 World Championship is an international motorcycle racing series organized by the Fédération Internationale de Motocyclisme (FIM). The series consists of multiple Grand Prix events held on renowned circuits worldwide, including iconic locations such as Italy, Japan, and Spain.

The series features a single class, Moto2, where all bikes use 765cc 4-stroke engines supplied by a single manufacturer. 

Moto2 serves as a feeder series to the MotoGP series.

details.name: Moto3
details.description:
The Moto3 World Championship is an international motorcycle racing series run by the Fédération Internationale de Motocyclisme (FIM). The series consists of races held on various circuits around the world, typically spanning around 40 minutes per race. This class features 250cc four-stroke motorcycles and is known for its close and competitive racing.

Currently, the series runs with a single class, where all motorcycles adhere to the same technical specifications to ensure a level playing field. The championship is a proving ground for young and upcoming riders, with many using it as a stepping stone to higher classes like Moto2 and MotoGP.

details.name: MotoGP Rookies
details.description:
The MotoGP Rookies Cup is an international motorcycle racing series designed to discover and nurture young talent in the sport. Run by Dorna Sports and the Red Bull organization, the series consists of races held on various circuits around the world, often alongside MotoGP events. The races typically span around 30 minutes each and feature riders aged 13 to 17.

The series runs with a single class, where all competitors ride identical KTM RC 250 R motorcycles, ensuring that the competition focuses on rider skill and development.

details.name: World SBK
details.description:
The MOTUL FIM Superbike World Championship is the global pinnacle of production-based motorcycle road racing and now in its 39th season. The 2026 grid features 22 riders across 14 teams running 1000cc machines from Ducati, BMW, Yamaha, Honda, Kawasaki and Bimota, contesting 12 European, Australian, Asian and Middle Eastern rounds with three races per weekend (Race 1 on Saturday, Tissot Superpole Race and Race 2 on Sunday) on Pirelli control rubber. 

The 2026 season opens a new chapter in the paddock - three-time and defending champion Toprak Razgatlıoğlu has departed for MotoGP with Pramac Yamaha (in a straight swap with Miguel Oliveira, now alongside Danilo Petrucci at ROKiT BMW), while six-time champion Jonathan Rea has retired into a Honda HRC test role. That leaves Aruba.it Racing's Nicolo Bulega as the overwhelming title favourite on the new-homologation Ducati Panigale V4 R, with Iker Lecuona joining him in factory red, Honda HRC fielding MotoGP convert Somkiat Chantra and ex-Moto2 racer Jake Dixon, and Stefano Manzi stepping up to GRT Yamaha after his 2025 WorldSSP title.

details.name: World SSP
details.description:
The FIM Supersport World Championship is WorldSBK's flagship support series and the traditional middleweight world title, sitting between WorldSBK and the new WorldSPB on the global ladder. Following the 2023 regulation overhaul that opened the class beyond the old 600cc-four formula, the 2026 grid features a record nine manufacturers - Yamaha, Ducati, Triumph, Honda, Kawasaki, MV Agusta, QJMOTOR, Kove and newcomer ZX Moto - with 33 riders across 21 teams competing on Pirelli tyres. The class allows larger displacement bikes from manufacturers including Ducati (Panigale V2), Triumph (Street Triple 765), MV Agusta (F3) and Yamaha (R9), all balanced via performance-equivalence regulations. 

The 2026 entry list reads strong: 2025 runner-up Can Öncü joins Pata Yamaha Ten Kate Racing (which secured its 11th Riders' title in 2025), two-time champion Dominique Aegerter returns to the class with Kawasaki, and ZX Moto's Valentin Debise has already made history by giving the Chinese marque its first WorldSSP wins at the season opener.

details.name: World SPB
details.description:
The FIM Sportbike World Championship is the newest class in the WorldSBK paddock, launched in 2026 as the long-term replacement for the WorldSSP300 class that ran from 2017 to 2025. The inaugural grid features 33 riders across 18 teams from 16 nationalities, with six manufacturers - Aprilia, Kawasaki, Kove, Suzuki, Triumph and Yamaha - competing on lightweight performance-balanced production sportbikes including the Yamaha R7, Kawasaki ZX-6R 636, Suzuki GSX-8R, Triumph Daytona 660 and Kove 450RR. 

The class is the world-championship equivalent of MSVR's hugely successful Pirelli British Sportbike (and similar series in Germany, Italy and Spain), and follows an eight-round calendar opening at Portimão on 27 March 2026. Notable names include two-time WorldSSP300 champion Jeffrey Buis on Suzuki, 2025 WorldSSP300 champion Beñat Fernández staying with Kove, ex-Moto3 race winner Xavi Artigas on Kawasaki, 2025 British Sportbike champion Kas Beekmans on Suzuki, and 2025 FSBK Yamaha Challenge 700 champion Gabin Cazard — and ZXMOTO's Frenchman Valentin Debise has already made an early statement with a double-win debut at the opener.

details.name: World WCR
details.description:
The FIM Women's Circuit Racing World Championship is the premier all-female motorcycle road racing series, launched in 2024 with the explicit mission of providing a global development platform for women in motorcycle sport. It is a strict single-make series in which all riders compete on identical, race-prepared Yamaha YZF-R7s producing around 80hp from the 689cc CP2 parallel-twin engine, with Pirelli Diablo Superbike control tyres, Panta Racing Fuel and centralised technical management by Luca Montiron's JiR organisation - keeping the focus squarely on rider talent. 

The 2026 calendar comprises six rounds run as support to the European legs of WorldSBK (Portimão, Misano, Assen, Donington Park, Balaton Park and Jerez), with two races per weekend and a 24-rider grid spanning 11 nationalities. Reigning 2025 champion Maria Herrera leads the field for Terra & Vita GRT Yamaha after her dramatic comeback title, with 2025 runner-up Beatriz Neila (Ampito Crescent Yamaha), Sara Sánchez, Roberta Ponziani, Chloe Jones and rookie sensation Paola Ramos all in podium contention - while 2024 inaugural champion Ana Carrasco's progression to full-time WorldSSP underlines WorldWCR's value as a genuine pathway to the wider WorldSBK paddock.

details.name: FIM EWC
details.description:
The FIM Endurance World Championship (EWC) is an international motorcycle racing series run by the Fédération Internationale de Motocyclisme (FIM). The series consists of endurance races, ranging from 8 to 24 hours, held on various circuits around the world, including iconic events like the Bol d’Or and the Suzuka 8 Hours.

Currently, the series features multiple classes, including the Formula EWC class, which consists of highly modified motorcycles designed for maximum performance, and the Superstock class, which features bikes that are closer to production models. Teams in both classes compete with a mix of professional and amateur riders, emphasizing both speed and reliability.

details.name: British Superbikes
details.description:
The British Superbike Championship is the UK's premier motorcycle road racing series and widely regarded as the strongest domestic superbike championship in the world. Now in its 30th season and rebranded as the ZYN British Superbike Championship for 2026 following the departure of long-term title sponsor Bennetts (with Carole Nash also coming aboard as official insurance sponsor), it is run by MotorSport Vision under the regulatory eye of the MCRCB and overseen by Series Director Stuart Higgs. 

The grid features 1000cc machines from Ducati, Bimota, BMW, Yamaha, Honda, Kawasaki and Suzuki, all competing on Pirelli control tyres across a 12-round, three-race-per-weekend calendar from Oulton Park in May through to Brands Hatch in October. The season culminates in the iconic three-round Showdown introduced in 2010, where the top eight riders fight for the title across the final seven races. Reigning champion Kyle Ryde, two-time champion Bradley Ray, three-time North West 200/Ulster GP-honed Glenn Irwin, Storm Stacey on the new Bathams AJN Racing Ducati Panigale V4 R, and Max Cook leading the new AJN Steelstock Bimota project all headline a stacked 2026 entry list.

details.name: British Supersport
details.description:
The Quattro Group British Supersport Championship is BSB's flagship support class and the traditional middleweight feeder series, having served as the proving ground for nearly three decades' worth of British and world champions. Following a major regulation overhaul in 2023, the class moved away from its 600cc-only roots and now welcomes a wider mix of production-based machinery - including the Triumph Street Triple 765 RS, Ducati Panigale V2, Yamaha R9, Honda CBR600RR, Suzuki GSX-R750 and Kawasaki ZX-6R - with manufacturer parity managed via balance-of-performance controls. 

The 2026 grid features five-time champion Jack Kennedy continuing with Honda Racing UK in pursuit of a record sixth crown, 2023 champion Ben Currie leading McAMS Yamaha's R9 charge alongside Harry Truelove, 2015 champion Luke Stapleford on the new Scars Racing Ducati, Bradley Perie on the Macadam Triumph, and reigning Australian Supersport champion Jack Mahaffy joining ASTRO-JJR HIPPO Suzuki - making it one of the deepest fields in years.

details.name: British Superstock
details.description:
The Pirelli National Superstock Championship is BSB's litre-class production series and has long been one of the most reliable talent pipelines into the main Superbike class - past graduates filling much of the current BSB grid. The class runs 1000cc production machines in close-to-stock specification, with strict limits on engine modifications and a controlled electronics package designed to keep budgets in check and racing tight; all bikes run on Pirelli control rubber. 

Defending champions MLav Racing return for 2026 with a new line-up of Lewis Rollo and Simon Reid on Motorrad Central Edinburgh BMW M 1000 RRs, while established names like Luke Mossey (Milenco by Padgetts Motorcycles) head a competitive grid that typically draws 25–30 riders across all 12 rounds.

details.name: British Sportbike
details.description:
The Pirelli British Sportbike Championship is the newest class on the BSB platform and, despite only being in its third season, is already the most popular category in terms of grid numbers. Introduced in 2024 as a replacement for the old Junior Superstock class, it caters for sub-supersport machinery with a 95hp performance equivalence, allowing models from Aprilia, CF Moto, Kawasaki, Kove, Suzuki, Triumph and Yamaha to compete on a level technical footing - all bikes running a unified Mektronic ECU, balance-of-performance controls and Pirelli slicks. 

MSVR has led the global rollout of the concept, with sister series now established in Germany, Italy and (from 2026) Spain, and the FIM is launching a World Sportbike Championship for 2026 — which is why the UK series has been rebranded from "National" to "British" Sportbike to align with international naming conventions. Richard Cooper took the inaugural 2024 title.

details.name: American Superbikes
details.description:
The MotoAmerica Superbike Championship is a premier motorcycle racing series in the United States, sanctioned by the American Motorcyclist Association (AMA) and FIM North America. The series consists of races held on various circuits across the country, typically spanning around 30 to 40 minutes per race. MotoAmerica aims to promote and develop motorcycle road racing in the United States while providing thrilling and competitive racing action.

Currently, the series features multiple classes, including the premier Superbike class, which showcases high-performance, production-based motorcycles, and support classes such as Supersport, Stock 1000, Twins Cup, and Junior Cup. These classes cater to a broad range of talent, from emerging riders to experienced professionals.

details.name: American Supersport
details.description:
The MotoAmerica Supersport Championship is a prominent motorcycle racing series in the United States, sanctioned by the American Motorcyclist Association (AMA) and FIM North America. This series runs alongside the MotoAmerica Superbike Championship, featuring races held on various circuits across the country, typically spanning around 30 minutes per race. The Supersport class is specifically designed to showcase middleweight, production-based motorcycles, emphasizing close and competitive racing.

Currently, the MotoAmerica Supersport Championship features motorcycles with engine capacities ranging from 600cc to 750cc, attracting both emerging talents and experienced riders.

details.name: King of the Baggers
details.description: The King of the Baggers is a unique motorcycle racing series in the United States, organized by MotoAmerica. It features heavily modified touring motorcycles, commonly known as baggers, competing on various circuits across the country. The races typically span around 30 minutes and showcase the performance and handling of these large, production-based bikes. Known for its exciting and unconventional racing, the King of the Baggers series attracts both riders and fans with its distinctive blend of speed and spectacle.

details.name: Twins Cup
details.description: The MotoAmerica Twins Cup is a competitive motorcycle racing series in the United States, sanctioned by the American Motorcyclist Association (AMA). This series features lightweight, twin-cylinder motorcycles, providing a platform for close and exciting racing. Held on various circuits across the country, races typically span around 30 minutes. The Twins Cup is known for its accessibility to emerging talents and its emphasis on rider skill and machine performance, making it a popular and integral part of the MotoAmerica racing program.

details.name: Moto America Talent Cup
details.description: The Mission Super Hooligan National Championship (SHNC) is one of the most distinctive support classes in MotoAmerica's AMA Superbike paddock, created by custom builder Roland Sands Design in conjunction with MotoAmerica to bring naked bikes, streetfighters and big-bore twins onto a national road racing grid. Eligible machines must be production-derived, water- or air-cooled twin-cylinder four-strokes of 750cc or more, with stock frames, upright (top-mounted riser) handlebars, no fairings, a 420lb minimum weight and a stock claimed output capped at 128hp. Bikes commonly seen on the grid include the Harley-Davidson Pan America, Indian FTR1200, KTM 890 Duke R, Ducati Hypermotard, Yamaha MT-09 and Suzuki GSX-S1000, while electric machinery from the likes of Zero and Lightfighter is also eligible — making it one of the few national series where ICE and EV bikes share the same grid.

details.name: Super Hooligan
details.description: The Parts Unlimited Talent Cup By Motul is MotoAmerica's youth development class, launched in 2025 as the official American feeder series into Dorna's Road to MotoGP™ programme. It's a strict spec series open to riders aged 14 to 21, all of whom compete on the purpose-built Krämer APX-350 MA - a lightweight GP-style machine powered by a 350cc single-cylinder engine derived from the KTM EXC-F enduro unit, producing more than 55hp and tipping the scales at under 249lbs ready to race. The bike runs a chromium-molybdenum trellis frame, Mectronik MKE7 race ECU with ride-by-wire throttle, WP Suspension, Brembo Stylema brakes and Dunlop slicks, and is priced at $28,995 for 2026 - a deliberately affordable platform aimed at giving young American racers true grand prix-style equipment.

details.name: French Superbikes
details.description: The Superbike class is the headline category of the Championnat de France Superbike (FSBK) and the highest level of national road racing in France. Run under the auspices of the Fédération Française de Motocyclisme (FFM), it features production-derived 1000cc machines from manufacturers including BMW, Honda, Yamaha, Ducati and Kawasaki, with riders battling across two races per weekend at six rounds. The 2026 calendar takes in Le Mans, Lédenon, Nogaro, Pau-Arnos, Le Vigeant and Magny-Cours, with the season finale at Magny-Cours doubling up with the French Promosport Cups for the first time.

details.name: French Supersport
details.description: The Supersport class sits directly below Superbike in the FSBK hierarchy and serves as the primary stepping stone for riders aiming at the top of French and international competition. The category was overhauled in 2024 to align with World Supersport regulations, opening the door to new manufacturers and 600cc machines with significantly modified engines — the Yamaha R6 unit, for example, jumping from around 140hp to roughly 160hp under the revised rules. Bikes such as the Yamaha R9, Ducati Panigale V2 and Triumph Street Triple 765 R are now eligible alongside more traditional 600cc fours.

details.name: French Moto 4 OGP
details.description: Moto 4 OGP by Motul is the youth feeder class within the FSBK and a key rung on the FFM's Filière Vitesse pathway, designed to funnel young French talent towards Moto3 and ultimately MotoGP. The category has been restructured for 2026: the former Objectif Grand Prix (OGP) by Motul has merged with Moto 4 to become a single one-make cup based on the Honda NSF250R Moto3-spec machine. This consolidation gives young riders a consistent platform to develop on circuit-style 250cc four-stroke prototypes that closely mirror what they'll find on the world stage.

details.name: French Moto 5
details.description: Moto 5 is one of the entry-level junior classes within the FSBK structure, sitting alongside Moto 4 as part of the FFM's coordinated youth development ladder. The class is aimed at the youngest competitors progressing into circuit racing and is built around small-capacity, entry-level machinery designed to teach race craft, line discipline and tyre management before riders step up to the more powerful prototype-style equipment found in Moto 4 OGP.

details.name: French Sidecars
details.description: Sidecar racing is one of the FSBK's most spectacular categories, with three-wheeled outfits and their passengers leaning, hanging and crawling around the bodywork through every corner. Following the integration of the former Promosport sidecar championship into the FSBK for 2026, the class now runs three divisions: F600 (long-chassis outfits, 600cc fours plus the eligible Yamaha MT-09/R9, Kawasaki ZX-6R/636 and Triumph 675), F2 (short-chassis outfits) and a new Open sub-category covering older-generation 600–1000cc engines and pre-2004 carburettor units up to 1300cc. French titles are awarded in F600 and F2, while Open competes for a Coupe de France.

details.name: French Yamaha 500
details.description: The Yamaha Challenge 700 is a single-make brand cup launched within the FSBK in 2025 and renewed for 2026, run exclusively on the Yamaha R7 powered by the 689cc CP2 parallel-twin engine. Bikes must be owned by the rider and prepared with a defined list of approved parts from the Yamaha and GYTR catalogues — including an Akrapovič exhaust, Öhlins fork cartridges and rear shock, GYTR rearsets and quickshifter — keeping the racing tight and the technical playing field genuinely level. Michelin Power Performance slicks are mandatory, and IXON Vortex3 leathers form part of the championship's branded equipment programme.

details.name: Super Motocross
details.description:
The AMA Supercross Championship is a premier off-road motorcycle racing series in the United States, sanctioned by the American Motorcyclist Association (AMA). This series features races held in stadiums across the country, with tracks consisting of dirt, jumps, and obstacles, creating a challenging and thrilling environment. Races typically span around 20 minutes plus one lap.

Currently, the series includes multiple classes, such as the 450SX class for the most powerful bikes and experienced riders, and the 250SX class for emerging talents.

details.name: MXGP
details.description:
The FIM Motocross World Championship (MXGP) is the pinnacle of international motocross, sanctioned by the Fédération Internationale de Motocyclisme (FIM). The series takes place on outdoor dirt circuits around the world, featuring natural terrain, man-made jumps, and technical obstacles that test rider skill and endurance. Races are run as two motos, each lasting around 30 minutes plus two laps.

The championship is divided into classes, with MXGP showcasing the most powerful 450cc machines and elite riders, while MX2 highlights rising stars competing on 250cc bikes.

details.name: MX2
details.description:
The MX2 World Championship is the premier international motocross category for emerging talent, sanctioned by the Fédération Internationale de Motocyclisme (FIM). Races are held on outdoor dirt tracks across the globe, combining natural terrain with demanding jumps, ruts, and technical sections. Each event features two motos, typically lasting 30 minutes plus two laps.

Riders in MX2 compete on 250cc four-stroke machines, making the class a proving ground for the sport’s next generation of stars on their path toward the top-tier MXGP category.

details.name: World Rally Raid Champ
details.description:
The World Rally-Raid Championship is the premier international series for off-road endurance rally racing, bringing together the world’s top teams, drivers, and riders. Governed by the FIA and the FIM, it tests competitors on some of the most challenging terrain imaginable, ranging from desert dunes to rocky mountain tracks. Spanning multiple continents and featuring iconic events like the Dakar Rally, the championship pushes both man and machine to their absolute limits.

Beyond the thrill of intense competition, the World Rally-Raid Championship also represents cutting-edge innovation and teamwork. Top automotive and motorcycle manufacturers utilize these events as proving grounds for advancing engineering, reliability, and safety features. For fans and participants alike, it offers a unique blend of adventure, athleticism, and global camaraderie, capturing the spirit of exploration and resilience that defines the sport.

details.name: Isle of Man TT
details.description:
The Isle of Man TT (Tourist Trophy) is one of the most famous and challenging motorcycle racing events in the world. Held annually on the Isle of Man, typically in late May and early June, the TT features time-trial races on a 37.73-mile (60.72 km) circuit known as the Snaefell Mountain Course. This course includes public roads closed for the event, with a mix of narrow, twisty streets, high-speed straights, and challenging elevation changes.

The Isle of Man TT consists of various classes, including Superbike, Supersport, Superstock, and Sidecar categories, attracting top riders and teams from around the globe. Known for its high speeds and extreme danger, the event demands exceptional skill, bravery, and precision. The TT is a celebrated tradition in motorcycle racing, with a history dating back to 1907, and it continues to captivate racing enthusiasts with its unique blend of excitement and peril.

details.name: North West 200
details.description:
The North West 200 is one of the fastest and most prestigious motorcycle road races in the world, held annually on public roads in Northern Ireland, typically in mid-May. The event takes place on the famed "Triangle" circuit, an 8.9-mile (14.3 km) course connecting the coastal towns of Portrush, Coleraine, and Portstewart. The circuit is renowned for its long, sweeping straights - particularly along the Coast Road - where bikes regularly exceed 200 mph, making it one of the quickest closed-road circuits anywhere on Earth, alongside more technical sections through built-up areas with chicanes, hairpins, and demanding corners like Mather's Cross, Juniper Hill, and Magheraboy.

The North West 200 features a programme of races across Superbike, Superstock, Supersport, and Supertwin classes, drawing many of the same world-class road racers who compete at the Isle of Man TT and the Ulster Grand Prix. With a history stretching back to 1929, the event has become a cornerstone of the international road racing calendar, often serving as a prelude to TT fortnight. Known for its blistering pace, passionate local support, and the inherent risks of racing at such speeds on public roads, the NW200 combines tradition, spectacle, and danger in a way few motorsport events can match - regularly attracting crowds of well over 100,000 spectators to the north coast.

details.name: Goodwood
details.description:
The Goodwood events are a series of premier motorsport festivals held at the Goodwood Estate in West Sussex, England. These events celebrate the rich heritage and excitement of motor racing through various formats and activities.

The Goodwood Festival of Speed, held annually in late June or early July, is a major celebration of motorsport and car culture. It features a hill climb where historic and modern vehicles race up the Goodwood House hill, along with exhibitions, demonstrations, and appearances by famous drivers and significant cars from the history of motorsport.

The Goodwood Revival, taking place in September, focuses on the golden era of motorsport, particularly from the 1940s to the 1960s. Held at the Goodwood Circuit, the Revival features races with vintage cars and motorcycles. Attendees often dress in period attire, adding to the nostalgic atmosphere, and the event is renowned for its authenticity and celebration of classic racing.

The Goodwood Members’ Meeting, typically held in the spring, is exclusive to members of the Goodwood Road Racing Club and their guests. It features competitive races with historic and modern vehicles, as well as demonstrations and high-speed track action. The Members’ Meeting aims to recreate the camaraderie and spirit of Goodwood’s original members’ meetings from the 1950s and 1960s.

details.name: Ginetta Challenge North America
details.description:
The Ginetta Challenge North America is a one-make racing series that features the Ginetta G56 GTA as its exclusive vehicle. This purpose-built race car, devoid of driver aids like ABS and traction control, emphasizes driver skill and vehicle dynamics. 

The series operates in collaboration with organizations such as the Sportscar Vintage Racing Association (SVRA) and National Auto Sport Association (NASA), providing a platform for both amateur and seasoned drivers to engage in competitive racing across various renowned circuits.

details.name: Ginetta GT Championship
details.description:
The Ginetta GT Championship is the top rung of the Ginetta GT ladder. Organised by Ginetta, the series travels with the British GT Championship, providing part of the ladder for GT drivers.

The cars are based on the Ginetta G56, in GTP and GT5 specification.

details.name: Ginetta Junior Championship
details.description:
The Ginetta Junior Championship is a premier junior racing series in the United Kingdom, organised by Ginetta Cars. The series consists of sprint races, typically featuring multiple short races per weekend, and follows the British GT calendar.

The Ginetta Junior Championship runs a single class featuring Ginetta G40 Junior cars. This series is designed for young drivers aged 14 to 17, providing a crucial stepping stone for aspiring racers aiming to progress through the ranks of motorsport.

details.name: World Racing League
details.description: The World Racing League (WRL) is an amateur endurance racing series in the United States that offers a professional-level racing experience on an amateur budget. Founded with the goal of providing real, affordable, and competitive racing without compromise, WRL hosts endurance races lasting from 8 to 24 hours. These events feature a diverse range of vehicles, from garage-built cars to factory-built race cars, competing in a multi-class format.

details.name: BRSCC Mazda Clubman
details.description:
The Mazda Clubman Championship is a popular UK circuit racing series aimed at grassroots competitors, offering an affordable and competitive entry point into endurance-style motorsport. Open to a variety of Mazda MX-5 models, the championship provides close, evenly matched racing, making it an ideal platform for drivers to build experience and racecraft.

With its emphasis on accessibility and track time, the series has become a key stepping stone for club racers looking to progress within motorsport. Many competitors use the championship to develop their skills before moving into higher-level national series or more specialised endurance categories.

details.name: NHRA
details.description: The National Hot Rod Association (NHRA) is the premier drag racing organization in the United States, founded in 1951. It hosts events featuring high-powered cars racing over a quarter-mile strip, with categories including Top Fuel, Funny Car, Pro Stock, and Pro Stock Motorcycle. The NHRA season spans several months, culminating in the prestigious NHRA Nationals. Known for its intense speed and thrilling races, the NHRA is a cornerstone of American motorsport, attracting top drivers and passionate fans.

details.name: Formula Drift
details.description: Formula Drift, often abbreviated as Formula D, is a premier professional drifting series in the United States. Established in 2004, it features skilled drivers competing in high-powered cars, showcasing their ability to control slides at high speeds and angles. The series includes events across various iconic tracks, where drivers are judged on style, execution, and precision rather than speed alone. Known for its intense and thrilling competition, Formula Drift has grown into a major motorsport, attracting a large fan base and top drifting talent from around the world.

details.name: Drift Masters
details.description: Drift Masters, officially known as the Drift Masters European Championship (DMEC), is one of the premier professional drifting series in Europe. Founded in 2014, it brings together some of the world’s most skilled drivers to compete in high-horsepower machines, demonstrating precise car control through high-speed slides and aggressive angles. Unlike traditional racing, drivers are judged on style, execution, and proximity rather than who crosses the finish line first. Hosting events at iconic tracks and street circuits across Europe, Drift Masters has rapidly gained a global following and is now considered a top-tier championship in the drifting world.

details.name: FIA GT World Cup
details.description:
The FIA GT World Cup at Macau is a prestigious annual event that showcases the thrilling world of GT racing against the breathtaking backdrop of the Macau street circuit. Known for its challenging layout, the circuit combines high-speed straights with tight corners, demanding exceptional skill and precision from drivers. This unique setting not only tests the capabilities of the cars but also provides an exhilarating spectacle for fans, as drivers navigate the narrow streets and elevation changes, often at speeds exceeding 200 km/h. The race attracts a diverse lineup of top manufacturers and talented drivers from around the globe, making it a highlight of the motorsport calendar.

Since its inception, the FIA GT World Cup has garnered a reputation for fierce competition and dramatic racing. The event typically features a mix of established stars and emerging talents, all vying for victory in a high-stakes environment. Fans are treated to a thrilling show as teams employ advanced technology and strategic pit stops to gain an edge. The atmosphere in Macau during race week is electric, with a vibrant blend of motorsport enthusiasm and local culture, creating a unique experience for attendees. The GT World Cup not only celebrates speed and skill but also the spirit of racing, solidifying its place as a crown jewel in the global motorsport landscape.

details.name: FIA Formula Regional World Cup
details.description: The FIA Formula Regional World Cup is an exciting platform for aspiring racing drivers, serving as a crucial stepping stone in their careers as they transition from karting to higher levels of motorsport. This championship showcases the talents of young drivers competing in single-seater cars that adhere to FIA Formula Regional regulations, which emphasize a balance of performance and safety. The series is designed to provide participants with valuable experience on various circuits while fostering their skills in a competitive environment. By featuring events on renowned tracks around the world, the Formula Regional World Cup allows drivers to gain exposure and build their reputations within the motorsport community.

details.name: FIA F4 World Cup
details.description: The FIA Formula 4 World Cup at Macau represents a thrilling showcase for emerging racing talent, providing a key proving ground for drivers beginning their journey up the single-seater ladder. Serving as a bridge between karting and more advanced categories, this prestigious event highlights young competitors behind the wheel of FIA-compliant Formula 4 machinery — designed to balance performance, safety, and accessibility. Set against the backdrop of the legendary Guia Circuit, the World Cup offers invaluable race experience on one of the most challenging street circuits in global motorsport, helping drivers sharpen their skills under intense pressure.

details.name: Macau Motorcycle Grand Prix
details.description:
The Macau Motorcycle Grand Prix is one of the most thrilling and prestigious events in the world of two-wheeled racing, offering a challenging platform for elite riders to test their skills on the legendary Guia Circuit. Known for its demanding combination of fast straights, tight corners, and unforgiving barriers, the event stands as a true test of precision, courage, and control. The race attracts top talent from around the globe, including international road racing stars and superbike specialists, all competing in high-performance machines that showcase both rider ability and engineering excellence.

As a cornerstone of the Macau Grand Prix weekend, the Motorcycle Grand Prix holds a special place in motorsport history. It provides riders with invaluable experience on one of the most technical and iconic street circuits in the world, while giving fans a spectacular display of speed and skill. The event not only highlights the competitive spirit of motorcycle racing but also celebrates its heritage, bringing together diverse teams, riders, and fans in an atmosphere of excitement and respect. With its blend of danger, drama, and world-class competition, the Macau Motorcycle Grand Prix continues to be a defining fixture on the international motorsport calendar.

details.name: Macau Roadsport Challenge
details.description:
The Macau Roadsport Challenge is an exhilarating showcase of production-based racing, bringing together skilled drivers and finely tuned road cars to compete on the iconic Guia Circuit. Combining the thrill of street racing with the accessibility of road-legal performance machines, the event highlights the connection between everyday sports cars and high-level motorsport. Competitors face the demanding twists, tight corners, and high-speed sections of Macau’s legendary circuit, testing both their driving precision and the limits of their cars in one of the world’s most challenging urban racing environments.

As part of the prestigious Macau Grand Prix weekend, the Roadsport Challenge offers an exciting platform for talented drivers to demonstrate their ability in a competitive yet relatable format. The race attracts a diverse field of participants from across the region and beyond, showcasing a wide variety of marques and performance setups. With its focus on skill, strategy, and car control, the Macau Roadsport Challenge delivers intense racing action and serves as a celebration of performance driving at its purest—making it a favourite among fans and competitors alike.

details.name: Mini Challenge JCW
details.description: The MINI CHALLENGE JCW series is a prominent one-make racing championship in the UK, utilizing the MINI John Cooper Works (JCW) F56 model. Each vehicle is equipped with a 2.0-litre turbocharged engine, delivering approximately 255bhp, and features a Quaife sequential gearbox, three-way adjustable Nitron dampers, and Alcon brakes. The cars run on Goodyear slick and wet tyres, mounted on 17-inch Team Dynamics wheels. This series is part of the TOCA package, supporting BTCC.

details.name: Mini Challenge Trophy
details.description: The MINI CHALLENGE Trophy is a highly competitive one-make racing series in the UK, featuring the MINI Cooper R56 model. Each car is powered by a 1.6-litre naturally aspirated engine, producing approximately 130bhp, and is equipped with a Quaife ATB limited-slip differential, GAZ adjustable dampers, and performance brakes. The series runs on Goodyear slick and wet tyres, fitted to 15-inch Team Dynamics wheels. As an entry-level category within the MINI CHALLENGE UK, it provides a cost-effective platform for aspiring drivers to develop their racing skills.

details.name: BRSCC Fiesta Juniors
details.description:
The BRSCC Fiesta Junior Championship is a premier UK motorsport series designed for aspiring racers aged 14 to 17, providing an accessible platform to launch their circuit racing careers. Participants compete in Mk7 Ford Fiesta 1.6 Zetec S race cars, meticulously prepared to ensure safety and performance, making it an ideal environment for young drivers to develop their skills.

The championship has a strong track record of nurturing talent, with alumni progressing to higher levels of motorsport, including the British Touring Car Championship (BTCC).

details.name: FIA European Karting Championship
details.description:
The FIA Karting European Championship, officially known as the Mondokart.com FIA Karting European Championship due to sponsorship, is a premier kart racing competition organized by the CIK-FIA. It serves as one of the two major karting competitions sanctioned by the FIA, alongside the Karting World Championship. 

The championship has been a significant platform for drivers, with several past participants, including Michael Schumacher, Jenson Button, Lewis Hamilton, Sebastian Vettel, and Max Verstappen, progressing to win the Formula One World Drivers’ Championship.

details.name: Champions of the Future
details.description:
The Champions of the Future series, promoted by RGMMC, has become a pivotal platform in international karting since its inception in 2020. It offers young drivers a competitive environment to hone their skills, with many participants advancing to higher tiers of motorsport. 

Notably, the series has featured drivers like Enzo Tarnvanichkul, the 2022 CIK-FIA World Karting Champion in the OK-Junior category, and Fionn McLaughlin, who joined the Red Bull Junior Team in 2024 after impressive performances in karting championships.

details.name: IAME Eurocup
details.description: The IAME Eurocup is a premier international karting series that brings together top drivers from across Europe to compete at some of the continent’s most iconic circuits. Designed to foster talent and provide a pathway for career progression, the 2025 IAME Eurocup features seven competitive rounds, offering participants prestigious awards and opportunities to advance within the world of motorsport.

details.name: IAME Italy
details.description: The IAME Italy Championship is a premier national karting series that attracts top drivers from across Italy and beyond, competing at some of the country’s most renowned circuits. Focused on developing emerging talent and supporting progression through the motorsport ranks, the championship features a multi-round calendar of highly competitive racing, providing drivers with valuable experience, prestigious titles, and a strong platform to advance within the global motorsport ladder.

details.name: IAME UAE
details.description: The IAME UAE Championship is a leading regional karting series that brings together top drivers from across the Middle East and beyond to compete at some of the region’s premier circuits. Designed to nurture emerging talent and provide a clear pathway for career progression, the IAME UAE Championship features a multi-round calendar of highly competitive racing, offering drivers valuable experience, prestigious awards, and opportunities to progress within the global motorsport ladder.

details.name: Ultimate Karting Championship
details.description:
The Ultimate Karting Championship (UKC) is a prominent kart racing series in the United Kingdom, established to provide drivers with a competitive platform for career development. The 2025 UKC series is scheduled over seven rounds, offering participants prestigious awards and exclusive opportunities to advance their racing careers.

The championship maintains an active presence on social media platforms, including Instagram and Facebook, where updates, announcements, and multimedia content are regularly shared to engage the karting community.  Additionally, the UKC’s YouTube channel features race highlights and other related content, providing fans and participants with insights into the competition’s proceedings.  For detailed race results and championship standings, the UKC collaborates with Alpha Timing, ensuring accurate and timely information is accessible to all stakeholders.

details.name: 24h Kart Series
details.description:
The 24-hour karting series is a collection of endurance kart races held annually across Europe, challenging drivers to compete in continuous 24-hour events. Notably, Teesside Karting in Middlesbrough, UK, organizes the British 24hr race, which is recognized as one of the largest karting events globally, featuring over 80 teams on the grid.  Additionally, the series includes the prestigious Le Mans 24hr kart race in France, celebrated for its rich history and the opportunity it provides for both amateur and professional drivers to test their skills and endurance.

These races are renowned for their demanding nature, requiring teams to strategize driver changes, manage fatigue, and maintain consistent performance throughout the 24-hour period. The events attract a diverse range of participants, from seasoned racers to enthusiastic newcomers, all seeking the thrill of endurance racing. The 2025 series is set to commemorate 34 years of endurance racing, with the Le Mans event scheduled for 11-13 July and the British 24hr race from 8-10 August, promising unforgettable experiences for all involved.

details.name: Clio Cup Bohemia
details.description:
Clio Cup Bohemia is a one-make touring car racing series held in Central Europe, primarily featuring the Renault Clio Cup IV and V models. Organized by Krenek Motorsport, the championship offers a cost-effective platform for both emerging talents and seasoned drivers to compete on renowned circuits across the region.

The series comprises six race weekends, totaling 12 races, at notable venues such as the Red Bull Ring, Slovakia Ring, Poznań, Most, Brno, and Balaton Park . Each event follows a consistent format: a 30-minute practice session, a 20-minute qualifying session, and two 25-minute races. Notably, the second race features a partially reversed grid, adding strategic depth to the competition .

details.name: EnduroKa
details.description: EnduroKA, launched in 2019, is a UK-based endurance racing series that offers a cost-effective and enjoyable entry into motorsport. Participants compete using the Ford KA Mk1 1300cc models produced between 2002 and 2008, with minimal modifications allowed to ensure a level playing field. Standardized components include control suspension (coilovers), front brake pads, tyres, and roll-cage, all available through specified suppliers.

details.name: Motorsport Games GT
details.description: The FIA Motorsport Games is a biennial motorsport event administered by the Fédération Internationale de l'Automobile. It includes competitions from multiple motorsport disciplines and is entered by national motorsport authorities under their national flags.

details.name: Motorsport Games F4
details.description: The FIA Motorsport Games is a biennial motorsport event administered by the Fédération Internationale de l'Automobile. It includes competitions from multiple motorsport disciplines and is entered by national motorsport authorities under their national flags.

details.name: Motorsport Games TC
details.description: The FIA Motorsport Games is a biennial motorsport event administered by the Fédération Internationale de l'Automobile. It includes competitions from multiple motorsport disciplines and is entered by national motorsport authorities under their national flags.

details.name: Motorsport Games Rally
details.description: The FIA Motorsport Games is a biennial motorsport event administered by the Fédération Internationale de l'Automobile. It includes competitions from multiple motorsport disciplines and is entered by national motorsport authorities under their national flags.

details.name: Motorsport Games Other
details.description: The FIA Motorsport Games is a biennial motorsport event administered by the Fédération Internationale de l'Automobile. It includes competitions from multiple motorsport disciplines and is entered by national motorsport authorities under their national flags.

details.name: Motorsport Games Karting
details.description: The FIA Motorsport Games is a biennial motorsport event administered by the Fédération Internationale de l'Automobile. It includes competitions from multiple motorsport disciplines and is entered by national motorsport authorities under their national flags.

details.name: Le Mans Classic Series
details.description:
The racing series, which retraces the history of touring and endurance racing from 1950 to 2010 and includes all Peter Auto events such as Spa-Classic, Grand Prix de l’Age d’Or and Dix Mille Tours, is reinventing itself and will adopt a strong new name from 2026: Le Mans Classic Series.

This new identity fits in with the prestigious world of Le Mans Classic, further strengthening the identity and coherence of our competitions. Exclusive bonus: participants in the Le Mans Classic Series will have priority access to Le Mans Classic, if they present an eligible car. A real reward for the most loyal enthusiasts!

affiliate.ticketLinkTitle: 🇫🇷 Experience some of the greatest Le Mans cars ever made

details.name: GT3 Revival
details.description:
In collaboration with SRO Motorsports Group and Stéphane Ratel, Peter Auto is reviving the first generations of GT3s with the GT3 Revival Series, dedicated to models from 2006 to 2013.

Immerse yourself in the golden age of GT3, on Europe’s most iconic circuits, in the elegant and authentic spirit of Peter Auto. The perfect blend of competition, passion and heritage.

details.name: Legends of Le Mans
details.description:
In partnership with the ACO and Le Mans Endurance Management, Peter Auto launches Legends of Le Mans, a new series dedicated to the iconic LMP1s, LMP2s and GTs of the 2010s, models starting post Endurance Racing Legends (ERL).

A unique opportunity for gentlemen and gentlewomen drivers to get these legendary cars back on the track, in the prestigious setting of the WEC and Le Mans Classic meetings. An exceptional project for exceptional cars.

details.name: Shenington Kart Racing Club
details.description:
Shenington Kart Racing Club is one of the UK’s most respected karting venues, known for its dedication to grassroots motorsport and nurturing future racing talent. The club offers a vibrant platform where young drivers and seasoned karters compete on a historic circuit with a rich racing legacy.

Each race meeting blends close, competitive racing with a welcoming, community-driven atmosphere that embodies the true spirit of British karting. From Bambinos to Seniors, Shenington hosts a wide range of classes, giving drivers of all ages and abilities the chance to shine on one of the country’s most iconic tracks.

Events at Shenington are organised with precision and passion, drawing in families, hobbyists, and future stars of the sport. With its strong reputation for quality racing and commitment to the development of drivers, Shenington Kart Racing Club remains a cornerstone of UK karting.

details.name: World Desert
details.description:
The World Desert Championship is the premier international series for endurance racing across the world’s toughest deserts, uniting elite teams, drivers, and riders. Overseen by leading motorsport authorities, it challenges competitors with extreme environments, from endless sand dunes to unforgiving rocky landscapes. Spanning multiple continents and featuring landmark desert rallies, the championship pushes both human endurance and machine performance to the very edge.

Beyond the adrenaline of high-stakes competition, the World Desert Championship is also a showcase of innovation and collaboration. Top manufacturers use these grueling events as testing grounds for advancements in engineering, durability, and safety. For fans and competitors alike, it delivers a rare fusion of adventure, athleticism, and global spirit—capturing the essence of resilience and exploration that defines desert racing.

details.name: Scottish Mini Cooper Cup
details.description:
The Scottish Mini Cooper Cup is one of Scotland’s most exciting and accessible racing championships, built around close, competitive action and a shared passion for motorsport. Showcasing identical Mini Cooper race cars, the championship places the focus firmly on driver skill, delivering tight grids and wheel-to-wheel racing that has become a hallmark of the series. Contested primarily at Knockhill Racing Circuit, the championship has grown into a fan favourite within the Scottish motorsport scene.

Since its inception, the Scottish Mini Cooper Cup has provided a clear and competitive pathway for aspiring drivers, from newcomers entering their first races to experienced competitors refining their craft. The championship is known for its strong paddock atmosphere, where camaraderie and competition go hand in hand, and where drivers, teams, and supporters form a close-knit community.

With an emphasis on affordability, parity, and driver development, the Scottish Mini Cooper Cup continues to play a key role in nurturing talent within Scottish motorsport. It offers a proven platform for drivers aiming to progress into higher categories, while maintaining the spirit of grassroots racing that makes it one of the most respected and enjoyable championships in the UK.

details.name: Scottish C1 Cup
details.description:
The Scottish C1 Cup is a unique and rapidly growing endurance racing championship that brings a fresh, accessible approach to motorsport in Scotland. Built around the humble Citroën C1, the series transforms everyday road cars into competitive race machines, delivering thrilling, close-fought action with a strong emphasis on teamwork, strategy, and consistency. Raced primarily at Knockhill Racing Circuit, the championship has become a standout feature of the Scottish club racing calendar.

Designed to be one of the most cost-effective ways into endurance racing, the Scottish C1 Cup attracts a diverse mix of drivers—from complete beginners and track-day enthusiasts to experienced racers looking for something different. With multiple drivers sharing each car, the format encourages collaboration and creates a dynamic race environment where pit stops, driver changes, and race management play a crucial role in success.

Beyond the racing itself, the championship is known for its welcoming paddock and community-driven atmosphere. Teams often build and run their cars themselves, fostering a hands-on spirit that captures the essence of grassroots motorsport. The Scottish C1 Cup continues to grow in popularity, offering an inclusive, competitive, and highly enjoyable entry point into endurance racing while maintaining the core values of accessibility and fun.

details.name: Formula Ford Festival
details.description: The BRSCC Formula Ford Festival is one of the world’s greatest motorsport competitions and an event that has stood the test of time for decades, with the 2025 edition set to be its 54nd running and the 50th to do so at Brands Hatch in Kent. It’s seen many great and lauded drivers pass through on their way to great achievements in their racing careers and anyone who has been lucky enough to win is automatically recognised as a talented racer.

details.name: Walter Hayes Trophy
details.description: Since 2001 the Walter Hayes Trophy has provided some of the the very best racing seen here at the ‘Home of British Motor Racing’ on an annual basis. Formula Ford drivers from around the world descend on Silverstone every November to compete in the Walter Hayes Trophy, an event that provides a thrilling end to the Silverstone season.

details.name: Silverstone 24H
details.description:
The 2026 Silverlake Silverstone 24H marks a major evolution for the event, introducing an expanded multi-class format designed to create one of the UK’s largest and most inclusive endurance races. UK-specification Citroën C1 Racing Club cars will once again form the backbone of the event and are expected to make up the largest class on the grid, continuing their established role at the heart of the Silverstone 24 Hour Race. Alongside them, the 2026 class structure will also include BRSCC CityCar Cup cars, BMW 116 Endurance cars, Ford KA Endurance cars, European-specification Citroën C1s, plus an Open Class for comparable production-based vehicles that align with the spirit and intent of the event. The expanded format is designed to deliver close, competitive racing across multiple categories while maintaining the accessible, cost-controlled ethos that has become synonymous with the Silverstone 24H.

Set across three days of on-track action at Silverstone Circuit, the 2026 Silverlake Silverstone 24 Hour Race promises to be a major highlight of the endurance racing calendar. The event also welcomes the return of long-time supporters of C1 endurance racing, Silverlake Automotive Recycling, as headline sponsors for 2026. The Silverlake race team will once again provide extensive on-site support throughout the event, bringing a comprehensive range of quality-graded, warranty-assured recycled parts for multiple classes, alongside tyre fitting facilities operating for the full duration of the demanding 24-hour race.

details.name: SailGP
details.description: SailGP is an international sailing league featuring national teams racing identical high-performance F50 foiling catamarans in short, fast, stadium-style events. Established in 2018, it runs multiple global rounds each season, with points determining finalists who compete for a championship title and prize money.

details.name: Historic Sportscar Racing
details.description:
Historic Sportscar Racing (HSR) is an international motorsport organisation dedicated to the preservation, competition, and celebration of historic and vintage racing cars. Founded in the early 1980s, HSR sanctions and promotes professionally run race events featuring classic sports cars, GT cars, prototypes, and touring cars from the 1950s through to the early 2000s, all competing in carefully regulated classes that maintain historical authenticity while ensuring safe, competitive racing.

HSR operates a wide-ranging calendar of events across North America and internationally, often appearing at iconic circuits and alongside major motorsport weekends. Its championships and invitational races attract a global community of collectors, professional drivers, and amateur racers, with an emphasis on respectful competition, period-correct presentation, and the continued use of historically significant machinery. Through its events, HSR plays a key role in keeping motorsport history alive by allowing legendary cars to be raced as they were originally intended.

details.name: FIA European Truck Racing
details.description:
The European Truck Racing Championship (ETRC) is the FIA-sanctioned European championship for circuit racing trucks. Founded in 1985, it features purpose-built race trucks based on production commercial vehicles and built to a common technical and safety regulations framework.

The championship runs a multi-round calendar at permanent racing circuits across Europe. Entries include manufacturer-supported and independent teams using trucks from MAN, Scania, Volvo, IVECO, and DAF. Drivers compete in multiple races per event across defined classes, with regulations intended to control performance and costs.

details.name: British Truck Racing
details.description:
The British Truck Racing Championship is the UK’s premier motorsport series for heavyweight racing trucks, dedicated to delivering close, competitive, and highly entertaining circuit racing using purpose-built race trucks derived from production commercial vehicles. Established in the 1980s, the championship showcases machines from manufacturers such as MAN, Scania, Volvo, and DAF, all prepared to strict technical regulations that ensure parity, safety, and thrilling on-track battles.

The championship operates a national calendar across major UK circuits, often as part of large race weekend events, bringing truck racing to a wide and enthusiastic audience. Its grid features a mix of experienced professionals and emerging talent, competing across multiple classes that accommodate different specifications and budgets while maintaining strong competition throughout the field. Known for its distinctive spectacle, accessibility, and close racing, the British Truck Racing Championship plays a vital role in the UK motorsport landscape, combining raw power with precision driving and a strong paddock community.

details.name: Bathurst 6 Hours
details.description:
The Bathurst 6 Hour is an Australian endurance race held annually at Mount Panorama Circuit. Organised by Australian Racing Group, the event runs over a six-hour duration and features production-based cars. The race forms part of the Bathurst 6 Hour event weekend and is open to a range of vehicles homologated from road-going models.

The field is split into multiple classes based on performance and engine capacity, including categories for high-performance sports cars, turbocharged vehicles, and naturally aspirated production cars. Entries typically consist of two to four drivers per car, with a mix of professional and amateur competitors. The race includes pit stops for fuel, tyres, and driver changes, with results determined by overall classification and class finishes.

details.name: Stadium Super Trucks
details.description:
In collaboration with leading promoters and circuits around the world, Stadium Super Trucks delivers a unique form of racing built on action, unpredictability, and spectacle.

Experience the raw intensity of off-road racing brought into stadiums and street circuits, where identical trucks launch over ramps, battle wheel-to-wheel, and defy gravity on every lap. A fast-paced format, close competition, and high-impact moments combine to create one of motorsport’s most distinctive and entertaining series.
