featured.name: Ultimate Karting Championship
featured.description: The Ultimate Karting Championship (UKC) is a prominent kart racing series in the United Kingdom, established to provide drivers with a competitive platform for career development. The 2025 UKC series is scheduled over seven rounds, offering participants prestigious awards and exclusive opportunities to advance their racing careers.
featured.descriptionTwo: The championship maintains an active presence on social media platforms, including Instagram and Facebook, where updates, announcements, and multimedia content are regularly shared to engage the karting community.  Additionally, the UKC’s YouTube channel features race highlights and other related content, providing fans and participants with insights into the competition’s proceedings.  For detailed race results and championship standings, the UKC collaborates with Alpha Timing, ensuring accurate and timely information is accessible to all stakeholders.

featured.name: Michael Williams
featured.description:
Michael began his racing career in 2023, competing in a partial season of the Radical Cup UK. A lifelong motorsport enthusiast, his passion for racing was sparked at a young age, inspired by watching his uncle compete in Radical championships over several years.

During his debut season, he gained valuable experience at some of the UK’s most iconic circuits, including Silverstone - the home of British motorsport.
featured.descriptionTwo:
Michael has since progressed into the Scottish Mini Cooper Cup, where he secured an impressive 5th-place championship finish last season. Now entering his third year in the series, he is aiming to build on that success and establish himself as a consistent front-runner.

With ambitions to challenge for the championship this year, he is also setting his sights on the next step in his career - a move into the Mini Challenge - with the long-term goal of competing in the British Touring Car Championship (BTCC).

featured.name: Daniella Sutton
featured.description:
Daniella Sutton, a young female racing driver, and a 2025 Junior British Racing Drivers Club (BRDC) Rising Star. She is with company such as Lewis Hamilton, so being selected as a Junior Rising Star, there is no better company to be in! Daniella has had a successful karting career, with many accolades including being crowned Total Karting Zero UK North Champion in 2022.

Daniella won the British Racing and Sports Car Club (BRSCC) Junior scholarship in 2024, against fierce competition of over 50 other drivers, in a 3-day-shoot out at Blyton Park, Lincolnshire. This gave Daniella the chance to step up from racing karts to cars in the BRSCC Junior Fiesta Championship.
featured.descriptionTwo: Daniella is a proud advocate for females in motorsport, and member of the British Women’s Racing Driver Club (BWRDC). Daniella a long term sufferer from Juvenile Idiopathic Arthritis (JIA) is an ambassador for The National Rheumatoid Arthritis Society, with aims to take the steps into Formula 4 for 2026 and showing young sufferers they can make their dreams happen.

featured.name: We Racing
featured.description:
At We Racing, our mission is to break barriers and set new standards in the racing world by embracing diversity, fostering talent, and driving excellence on and off the track. We believe that racing thrives when fuelled by diverse perspectives, and we are committed to creating an inclusive environment that champions the development of every team member.

With relentless determination, cutting-edge innovation, and a focus on continuous growth, we aim to not only win races but to elevate the sport, inspiring the next generation of racers from all walks of life.

featured.name: Jamie Caudle
featured.description:
As a dedicated racing driver and trained mechanic, Jamie has built a motorsport career rooted in a deep passion for both the technical and competitive sides of racing. In 2025, Jamie finished 2nd in the Ginetta GT Academy, after a close title fight that went down to the last weekend.. This experience not only reinforced his commitment to the sport but also gave him valuable insights into vehicle performance and race strategy.

With a strong background in mechanics, Jamie has a unique ability to understand and fine-tune his car on a deeper level, allowing him to maximise its performance and gain a competitive edge during races.
featured.descriptionTwo:
Looking ahead, Jamie has his sights firmly set on the global stage, with ambitions to compete in the FIA World Endurance Championship (WEC) and ultimately take part in the legendary 24 Hours of Le Mans. The demanding nature of endurance racing—testing both driver skill and mechanical resilience—is a challenge he is eager to take on. Driven by ambition and a relentless pursuit of excellence, Jamie is determined to make his mark in the world of motorsport.

For 2026, Jamie has stepped up into the Ginetta GT Championship.

featured.name: Henry Foote
featured.description:
Henry is a Degree Apprentice from Cornwall with a passion for motorsport and the importance of good mental health in our always-on, always available world. A few years ago, Henry found his mental health was being impacted and was incredibly fortunate to find motorsport, karting at first, as a break from the bits of life that were causing struggles.

Recognising the positive impact on himself but also that motorsport won’t be the answer for everyone, Henry believes the core of what benefitted him was finding a sporting passion, a new focus and a break from what was causing struggles was the key to returning to feeling well.
featured.descriptionTwo:
Henry is also involved in raising awareness for Scotty’s - a charity dedicated to supporting bereaved military families. A link to the charity can be found below.

For 2026, Henry is racing in the MINI CHALLENGE Trophy championship having competed as a rookie in 2025. Selected weeks will be streamed live in The Racing Line.

featured.name: TWM
featured.description: Welcome to TWMotorsport Kart Racing Team, where we strive to get the maximum performance from both Driver and Kart. Our passion to succeed has seen us win: Multiple Club Championships, Multiple English & ‘O’ Plate Championships, Numerous TKM Festival Winners, and British Kart Championship Team and Drivers Championships.
featured.descriptionTwo:
At TWMotorsport, we offer a wide range of services catering to all levels of karting enthusiasts. Whether you’re an absolute beginner eager to experience the thrill of karting for the first time or an aspiring champion with dreams of British Championships, we have you covered.

Our team of experienced professionals is not only dedicated to helping you achieve your racing goals but also to fostering a sense of camaraderie and sportsmanship within our karting community. We believe that karting is more than just a sport; it’s a way of life, a passion, and a journey that unites individuals with a shared love for speed and competition.

featured.name: IAME Italy
featured.description: IAME Series Italy is a premier kart racing championship in Italy.
featured.descriptionTwo:
The 2026 championship runs over multiple rounds, with race events held at Italy’s leading karting circuits, including South Garda Karting, Kartodromo Val Vibrata, Circuito Internazionale Napoli, and 7 Laghi Kart.

In addition to the national championship, IAME Italy drivers also compete in key international events, including the IAME Euro Series and the IAME International Final, alongside various national cups and preparatory events throughout the season.

The top finishers earn the opportunity to represent Italy at the IAME World Final, competing against the best IAME drivers from around the world.

featured.name: IAME UAE
featured.description: IAME Series UAE is a premier kart racing championship in the United Arab Emirates.
featured.descriptionTwo:
The 2025-2026 championship runs over 10 rounds with race events taking place at all main tracks across the UAE - RAK Track, Dubai Kartdrome, Sharjah Kart Track, Al Forsan and Yas Marina Circuit.

In addition to the 10-round UAE championship, our IAME powered racers also compete in the annual IAME Middle East Cup, IAME Summer Series and Pre-Season Cup.

The top  finishers win  an entry to represent the country at the IAME World Final 2026.

featured.name: Radical World Finals
featured.description: Radical Motorsport is one of the world’s most prolific race car manufacturers, with over 3,000 cars produced over nearly three decades. From its 1997 inception, Radical set out to create a customer-led niche in the world of motorsport, a race-bred thrill-a-minute experience on the racetrack. Over the years Radical has cemented it’s place in motorsport history, from setting Nürburgring records to competing at the prestigious 24 Hours of Le Mans.
featured.descriptionTwo:
A combination of lightweight construction, superbike-derived technology and aerodynamic downforce delivers performance faster than a supercar with a driver-focused and visceral driving experience. Now in its third decade, the Radical stable includes the entry-level SR1 XXR, the best-selling SR3 XXR alongside the turbo-charged SR10 XXR, and the iconic RXC. All models are solely designed, engineered and built at Radical’s Cambridgeshire headquarters in England. A global dealer network spanning over 20 countries continues to grow, and there are now multiple single-make Radical Cup championships across four different continents, including the Radical Cup North America which races alongside several IndyCar weekends, the long-standing Radical Cup UK, and new for 2026 the Radical Cup Europe supporting the popular Creventic 24H Series.

Following the success of the previous Radical World Finals events in Las Vegas (2022), Portimão (2023), and Abu Dhabi (2024) the 2025 Radical World Finals will return to North America at Virginia International Raceway. Located in Alton, Virginia, Virginia International Raceway (VIR) is renowned for its challenging layout and picturesque setting. The 3.27-mile road course features high-speed straights, technical corners, and dramatic elevation changes, making it a favourite among drivers and spectators alike.

The Radical World Finals 2025 will feature four classes of competition. Drivers piloting the brand’s SR10 and SR3 race cars will vie for World Championship titles in their respective divisions, plus a new Gold class for drivers over the age of 30 competing in the competitive SR3 1500 class.

featured.name: Scottish Motor Racing club
featured.description:
The Scottish Motor Racing Club (SMRC), established in its current form in 1963, proudly organises and runs the prestigious Scottish Motor Racing Championships each year, which take place predominantly at Scotlands National Motorsport Centre; Knockhill Racing Circuit. In addition, the club organises a small number of away rounds each year to circuits such as Oulton Park, Anglesey, Croft, and Kirkistown in Northern Ireland. In 2025 the club has ran the Scottish MINI Cooper Cup, Scottish Fiesta ST Cup, Scottish C1 Cup and Scottish Classic Sports & Saloons.

The Scottish Mini Cooper Cup is recognised as one of the best single-make racing series in the UK. It has been a proving ground for many top drivers including British Touring Car Championship stars Rory Butcher, Aiden Moffat and Chris Smiley. Close, hard and competitive racing is guaranteed in this championship, which provides an ideal stepping stone for drivers looking to move up the motorsport ladder. Indeed, the championship takes its inspiration from the British Touring Car Championship in the way the races are formatted. Race 1’s grid is set by qualifying position while Race 2 is decided by the result of Race 1 and Race 3 is the wildcard – drawn by ballot with anywhere between the top 5 and 8 cars reversed. This format has proven to be highly effective in creating action packed and unpredictable races. Only the 1.6 Mini Cooper model built between 2001-2006 are eligible. The championship regulations are designed to ensure that cars are built to the same specification; the cars use identical Yokohama tyres and run the same ECU. This puts more emphasis on a driver’s skill by keeping the cars performance as equal as possible.
featured.descriptionTwo:
Fiesta racing has been a mainstay of Scottish Motor Racing for over 20 years, initially with the XR2 model – which is still raced in our Classics Sports and Saloons Championship – and more recently with the Fiesta ST model. The championship continues to offer incredibly close and hard racing at an affordable price point. The Fiesta ST race cars are powered by the standard but punchy 2 litre Duratec power unit which is found in the road going car. They run on the super grippy Yokohama A050 tyre and are equipped with ABS brakes and 3-way adjustable dampers. This makes for a responsive but forgiving driving experience that inspires confidence in the driver to push further and further towards the limit. For this reason, the championship attracts both seasoned veterans and newcomers alike. In the past, the Fiesta class has helped to launch the careers of some of Scotland’s top racing drivers (notably triple British Touring Car Champion, Gordon Shedden, started his motor racing career in the Fiesta championship) and today the championship still offers drivers a great apprenticeship in race craft and car control.

Launched in 2019, the Scottish C1 Cup, provides budget entry level racing in Scotland. To keep the racing exciting and the costs under control, the Citroen C1 Cup cars run to very simple but tight regulations. The race cars are almost literally a C1 road car with a roll cage bolted in, meaning that you can build a car – from start to finish – over the course of a weekend, if you’re in a rush! The C1’s 1 litre power unit may not make them the fastest race cars on a Scottish Championship race weekend but we guarantee that racing door handle to door handle around the humps and bumps of Knockhill racing circuit will bring a smile to any racers face – whether you’re a seasoned campaigner or a complete newcomer.

Always a crowd pleaser, the Scottish Classic Sports and Saloon Championship brings some nostalgia to the Scottish Championship. In many cases this championship offers drivers the opportunity to race the cars that they grew up watching and envying, but it also provides an avenue for anyone – who enjoys a passion for classic cars – to get out on track and enjoy the sheer pleasure of hustling iconic cars of yesteryear around the undulating Knockhill racing circuit. Both mass production-based cars and limited production cars can take part. The championship boasts 6 different classes which are mainly for cars manufactured up until 31st December 1989.  Additionally there is a seperate class for Ford Fiesta XR2 cars racing alongside the main championship cars and battling for the title of XR2 Champion.

featured.name: Dillon McKenna
featured.description:
Dillon is an Irish born Rally Co-Driver competing in the American Rally Championship (ARA) and Canadian Rally Championship (CRC). 

Raised in Ireland, a country with a strong rallying heritage, Dillon developed an early passion for the sport before relocating to Vancouver, Canada, following the completion of his university studies in Civil Engineering.
featured.descriptionTwo:
Dillon’s competitive rally career began after attending Dirtfish Rally School in Washington State where he was mentored by Alex Gelsomino (Ken Blocks Co-Driver) & Rhiannon Gelsomino (Travis Pastrana’s Co-Driver). In his debut ARA season, Dillon finished 2nd in the Limited 4WD Class (L4WD) and 4th overall in the Western Regional Championship aboard a Subaru WRX alongside Californian driver Robert Shibao. 

The 2025 season marked Dillon’s most successful year to date, winning L4WD class in both Eastern & Western Regional ARA Championships along with 2nd and 3rd overall in respective championships. 

Looking ahead to 2026, Dillon continues to race in ARA & CRC championships, with plans to also contest selected Irish and UK events.

featured.name: TCR Europe
featured.description:
TCR Europe was launched in 2016, and evolved into a fully-fledged international series in 2018, succeeding the FIA European Touring Car Championship/Cup as the major touring car championship across the European region.

Champions of the series include Aurélien Comte, Franco Girolami, Josh Files, Mehdi Bennani, Mikel Azcona, and Tom Coronel, and all have also had successful professional careers at a world championship level.
featured.descriptionTwo: The reigning champion is Jenson Brickley, who won a fully-paid entry in the 2026 Kumho FIA TCR World Tour as well as 100 Kumho race tyres after securing the title in his first full season in the series.

featured.name: TCR Europe Cup
featured.description: The TCR Europe Cup is new this year and is designed to help develop aspiring touring car racers, and make the jump to professional level racing. The Cup features a unique format, in which the car is shared across both an amatuer and professional level driver over the weekend, and races at two of Europe's most prestigious racing circuits, Spa-Francorchamps and Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya.

featured.name: Jude Peters
featured.description:
Jude Peters is an exciting young prototype driver. He drove a successful campaign in the Ligier European Series in 2025, finishing 3rd overall with numerous podiums and a win at Spa. In 2026 he’s stepping up to the Michelin Le Mans Cup, racing LMP3 cars for Team Virage. All this, less than two years since his first car race.

Jude’s path is anything but conventional. With no karting or academy background, he started on the sim. That sim-to-real transition has become a defining part of his story, and it has accelerated his progression through the ranks at remarkable speed.
featured.descriptionTwo:
Jude made his car racing debut after winning the prestigious Ginetta Junior Scholarship, immediately marking himself as a standout talent. He went on to win the Ginetta Junior Freshman Championship and is establishing himself as one of the most promising young drivers in this category. His ability to adapt, learn and deliver under pressure set the tone for what followed.

Now competing in LMP3 in the Michelin Le Mans Cup, Jude represents the next generation of endurance racing drivers, technically sharp, commercially aware and built for modern motorsport. Jude brings a compelling blend of performance, professionalism and a proven record of rapid progression. His trajectory toward the highest levels of endurance racing is already well underway.

featured.name: Jack Collins
featured.description:
Jack Collins is a British racing driver with over five years of competitive experience, whose motorsport journey began in karting at the age of eight. After progressing from an indoor members’ league to a professional karting team, he competed nationally in Junior X30 and Senior Rotax championships, developing strong racecraft, technical understanding, and driving fundamentals.

In 2025, Collins made the transition to car racing, testing the Ginetta GTP8 before joining E3Sport. Rapidly adapting to the car, he impressed during pre-season testing and carried that form into the championship, securing multiple class podiums and consistently strong race pace while refining his qualifying and tyre management.
featured.descriptionTwo:
His breakthrough came mid-season with his first pole position at Oulton Park, followed by dominant double pole positions and race victories at Brands Hatch and the final round at Donington Park. Collins finished the championship second overall in his debut car racing season, a notable achievement against far more experienced competitors.

For 2026, Collins steps up to the British GT Championship, competing in a BMW M4 GT4 with Century Motorsport alongside teammate Branden Templeton. Positive winter testing and the team’s professional, data-driven approach have reinforced his confidence, and he believes the package is capable of fighting for the championship in the highly competitive endurance series.

featured.name: Andrew Robinson
featured.description:
Andrew Robinson is an up-and-coming British racing driver making his mark in UK junior motorsport. At just 15 years old, he’s carved a path into competitive car racing after developing a strong passion for motorsport from a very young age, including early involvement in sim racing which helped shape his racecraft and competitiveness on track.

In 2025, Robinson stepped into the Ginetta Junior Championship with E3 Sport, one of the UK’s most respected junior racing outfits, contesting a partial season and gaining invaluable experience against a highly competitive field of drivers aged 14–17.
featured.descriptionTwo:
Building on that foundation, Robinson committed to a full season in the 2026 Ginetta Juniors with E3 Sport. The move underscores both his ambition and potential as he aims to fight closer to the sharp end of the grid, improve his race results, and continue his development through seat time and testing. 

Beyond his race results, Robinson has begun to attract support from partners and racing-related sponsors, reflecting growing confidence in his talent and future prospects in motorsport. As he continues in the Ginetta ladder, all eyes are on his continued growth behind the wheel.

featured.name: Oscar Stockley-King
featured.description:
Oscar Stockley-King is a 14-year-old racing driver competing in the Ginetta Junior Championship, widely regarded as the world’s leading junior touring car series. After rising quickly through the karting ranks and consistently demonstrating strong pace, Oscar has established himself as a promising young talent to watch.

With just two days of car testing under his belt, Oscar entered the highly competitive Ginetta Junior Scholarship - the most prestigious prize in UK motorsport - finishing inside the top 11. This performance confirmed his ability to compete at a high level and gave him the incentive to not let the opportunity slide.
featured.descriptionTwo:
His journey to the grid has been anything but straightforward. Balancing school commitments with his racing ambitions, Oscar personally sent over 600 tailored sponsorship proposals to potential partners. His determination and work ethic paid off, securing the support needed to take the next step in his career.

Now, stepping into the Ginetta Junior Championship in his Rabsport Ginetta G40 Evo with zero prior testing, Oscar faces a steep but exciting learning curve. This season is set to be a crucial period of development as he builds experience, refines his racecraft, and prepares for the challenges ahead in his motorsport journey.

featured.name: Le Mans Classic Series
featured.description:
The Le Mans Classic Series is a racing series organised by Peter Auto since 2004. Founded by Patrick Peter, the Peter Auto agency has specialised in organising historic motorsport events since the early 1980s. It is renowned for creating top-level circuit meetings, bringing together iconic endurance and touring cars on some of Europe’s most prestigious tracks. 

The Le Mans Classic Series races have become a global benchmark, offering a chance to relive the motorsport of a bygone era and the unique atmosphere that comes with it. The season consists of six rounds, culminating in the highlight of Le Mans Classic. Since the 2025 season, the races have been broadcast live, allowing enthusiasts and motorsport fans to enjoy spectacular racing cars from the 1950s through to the 2010s.
featured.descriptionTwo:
The Le Mans Classic Series races feature 10 grids of cars, each with its own distinct identity defined by a specific discipline and period. From Sports Prototypes to Touring Cars and Grand Tourers, spanning the 1950s to the 2010s, more than 350 cars take part across the meetings. 

At each round, competitors accumulate points that determine their ranking within their category (with up to five categories per grid). Today, the grids take turns on track during events held at some of Europe’s most renowned circuits, including Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya (Espíritu de Montjuïc), Spa Francorchamps (Spa Classic), Dijon-Prenois (Grand Prix de l’Âge d’Or), Circuit Paul Ricard (Dix Mille Tours), Autodromo Enzo e Dino Ferrari (Imola Classic), and Circuito do Estoril (Estoril Classic). 

On track, you will primarily see gentleman drivers going head-to-head, whether at the wheel of exceptional machines from the 1950s (Alfa Romeo TZ2, Ferrari 250 GT SWB, etc.) or prototypes from the golden age of ground effect (Peugeot 905 Evo, Ferrari 333 SP, etc.), all with a fierce competitive spirit. Each grid features private practice sessions, qualifying sessions and races, offering plenty of thrilling on-track battles — for example between BMW 3.0 CSLs and Ford Capri Zakspeeds — and a chance to relive historic motorsport, brought to life by commentators with encyclopaedic knowledge. 

The road to the podium begins with the very first race, and the pressure is ever-present when driving these fully mechanical machines without error. In the pit lane, our pit reporter takes you behind the scenes with live interviews of drivers during pit stops, while mechanics work tirelessly to find those crucial extra seconds and give both car and driver the best possible chance of securing a title.

featured.name: Thomas Holland
featured.description:
Thomas Holland’s journey into motorsport began in August 2017, when, at just 16 years old, he first drove a GT race car at Ginetta’s Blyton Park circuit as part of a summer school experience. What started as an early birthday present quickly turned into something much bigger, as he impressed immediately with lap times beyond expectations. Over the next few years, that initial spark developed into a serious pursuit, with Thomas building a strong racing foundation and rapidly progressing through the ranks.

Between 2020 and 2022, he competed in 94 races, finishing 92 of them and achieving podium results in over 39% of starts. His performance in GT machinery was particularly standout, with 49 race starts yielding 35 podiums (71%) and 7 wins, alongside just a single retirement. After finishing runner-up in the GT Academy championship in 2022, he attracted attention from multiple teams and secured several test opportunities ahead of the 2023 season.
featured.descriptionTwo:
Still only in his early twenties, Thomas earned FIA Silver status and became an ARDS-qualified instructor, balancing his racing career alongside a Mechanical Engineering degree at Swansea University. In 2023, he stepped up to British GT with support from Ginetta, making an immediate impact with a win on debut and another podium. He carried that momentum into 2024, winning his class at the 24H of Dubai following a last-minute call-up, before returning to British GT to secure a front-row start, a race win, two podiums, and fourth in the championship. He also impressed in GT Cup, taking two wins from just four races.

In 2025, Thomas expanded onto the European stage in McLaren Trophy Europe, adapting quickly to new circuits and machinery to deliver two wins and six podiums. Now 23, he continues his upward trajectory, and for 2026 he will compete in British GT with Innovation Racing in a Ginetta G56 GT4, aiming to build on his success and establish himself further in GT and endurance racing.

featured.name: Savva Solonchenko
featured.description:
USE CODE "SAVVA" FOR 30 DAYS FREE and 70% OFF

Savva Solonchenko is an emerging young talent in junior motorsport, steadily building his experience and reputation as he progresses through the ranks. With a clear focus on development and performance, he continues to take key steps toward a long-term racing career.

In 2025, Savva competed in the Ginetta Junior Winter Series, where he gained valuable track time and developed both his racecraft and technical understanding. The series provided a strong foundation, allowing him to refine his driving skills and prepare for the next stage of competition.
featured.descriptionTwo:
Following the winter campaign, Savva worked closely with Race Car Consultants through multiple test sessions, consistently showing strong pace and adaptability. These performances reinforced confidence from both his team and Malex Race Academy in his ability to succeed at the next level.

For 2026, Savva steps into the Fiesta Juniors championship with Race Car Consultants, marking an important progression in his career. With a competitive team behind him and continued support from Malex Race Academy, he looks set to develop further and establish himself as a driver to watch.

featured.name: Fgani Angelo Salubre
featured.description: Fgani Angelo Salubre is a Filipino racing driver progressing in European motorsport scene rooting his passion in motorsports from karting, F4 training and now in endurance race. He participated in Ligier JS Cup France in 2025 finishing 2nd place in Portimao with XP Racing team. This season he will be competing in GT Cup Open Europe 2026 with Hans Weijs Motorsport.

featured.name: Josh Hislop
featured.description:
Josh Hislop started his career in the Scottish Junior Rally Championship in 2015, collecting multiple podiums and stage wins before making the transition to circuit racing in the BRSCC Fiesta Junior Championship for 2017. He quickly established himself as a rising star of UK motorsport, winning the Fiesta Junior Rookie Championship and subsequently being awarded a place on Motorsport UK’s Talent Development Pathway.

Over the following years, Josh honed his craft in championships such as the Ginetta GT5 Challenge, SMRC MINI Cooper Cup and the Nurburgring Langstrecken Serie, before making the step to GT4 competition in the 2022 British Endurance Championship, claiming numerous podium finishes en-route to becoming Vice-Champion.
featured.descriptionTwo:
After three years away from the famous Green Hell, Josh returned to the Nurburgring Langstrecken Serie in 2024 and made an instant impression, dominating the hotly contested VT2-FWD category to take both the class championship and the Production Car Trophy Championship. His successes were rewarded in 2025, with a place on Manthey’s newly-founded Talent Pool Programme and an opportunity to step up to the Porsche Endurance Trophy Nurburgring’s Cayman GT4 class, where he went on to finish third in the ProAm championship.

For 2026, Josh continues his journey at the Nurburgring, taking on select rounds of the NLS with Breakell Racing in their Porsche Cayman GT4 CS and BMW M240i.

featured.name: Archie Clark
featured.description:
Archie Clark is a British racing driver progressing through the UK motorsport ladder, now competing in GT machinery and preparing for the next step in his career. After an early start in karting, Archie has built a strong reputation through consistent results and rapid progression, culminating in a move into car racing and multiple championship successes.

He began karting in 2016 and quickly found success, winning the 2017 MSA Bambino British Championship. Archie progressed through the cadet and junior ranks, collecting race wins and podiums before stepping up to junior competition. In 2021, he won the BKC Pooled Engine OKJ Championship and secured further strong results across national championships, alongside a race win on his Junior Rotax debut.
featured.descriptionTwo:
A breakthrough year followed in 2023, when Archie competed in European competition for the first time, finishing on the podium in three out of four rounds and placing sixth overall in the championship. He also qualified for the World Finals in Bahrain, racing through to the Grand Final and recovering from an early setback to finish 13th. That season, he was ranked third in the FIA Junior world rankings.

In 2024, Archie made the transition to car racing, competing in the Ginetta Junior Championship before stepping up again in 2025. Racing in both GT Cup with an Aston Martin Vantage GT4 and the Ginetta GT Championship, he won both championships in his first full season at that level. Archie now moves into the Porsche Carrera Cup Great Britain, continuing his progression toward top-level GT racing.

featured.name: Jack Davidson
featured.description:
Jack Davidson is a racing driver from Stirling, Scotland, competing in EuroNASCAR and continuing his progression in international stock car racing. He first made his mark in the NASCAR Whelen Euro Series, showcasing strong pace and racecraft against experienced grids.

Davidson made his series debut in 2023 with Marko Stipp Motorsport, where he quickly established himself as one to watch. He secured the Rookie Trophy overall title, finishing 7th in the championship standings with two top-five finishes and seven top-ten results across the season.
featured.descriptionTwo:
For 2026, Davidson returns to EuroNASCAR, stepping up to the newly renamed V8GP class. The move marks a significant step forward as he takes on a more competitive field and continues to build experience at a higher level within the championship.

With proven results in his debut season and a return to the grid, Davidson continues to establish himself as a rising Scottish talent in international stock car racing.

featured.name: Henry Howarth
featured.description:
While living in Australia, a six year old Henry got his first taste of motorsport in a tandem kart at Phillip Island Karting Track just outside Melbourne and was hooked from the moment that he climbed into the kart!

On return to the UK, at the age of eight, Henry began his motorsport career in indoor karting at Teamsport in Nottingham. It wasn’t until 2022 however that he graduated to outdoor owner karts and began to demonstrate good speed at Fulbeck in the Junior and later Senior Rotax classes. He also competed in Junior Rotax races at Tattershall, where he earned his maiden podium and first win.
featured.descriptionTwo:
He then decided to enter the Jamsport scholarship and a top 10 finish aided his decision to take the step up to car racing, the end of the 2023 season saw him compete in his first ever car race in the BRSCC Fiesta Junior Championship.

Henry then competed in the Jamsport 2024 scholarship and was very successful, leading him to upgrade his license and take on the senior championship. Having won the Am Class Championship in his first full year of racing, Henry is graduating to the JCW Sport category of Mini Challenge UK, a support series for the BTCC touring car weekends.

He currently attends Loughborough College on the Motorsport UK Academy DiSE course, a prestigious programme for the UK’s most promising racing drivers.

featured.name: David Perel
featured.description:
David Perel is a South African professional racing driver who competes for Ferrari customer GT teams across some of the most prestigious endurance championships in the world. His 2026 programme is his busiest to date, racing in the European Le Mans Series and Italian GT Championship with Spirit of Race, alongside GT World Challenge Europe and the ADAC 24H Nürburgring with Rinaldi Racing. His honours list includes two overall wins at the Spa 24 Hours, three Blancpain GT championships, race victories in both the European and American Le Mans Series, and Le Mans starts in 2021 and 2022.

His path to professional motorsport was anything but conventional. Perel started karting at 15, walked away from racing entirely at 23 when the funding ran dry, and only returned at 29 after watching Le Mans reignited a dream he had quietly carried for years. He turned professional at 32, making him a relative late bloomer in a sport that usually rewards drivers who are signed before they can drive a road car. That outsider's perspective shapes how he talks about racing today, both as a competitor and as a coach.
featured.descriptionTwo:
Sim racing has been woven through his entire career. Long before he was racing Ferraris in real life, Perel was using Gran Turismo to learn circuits he couldn't afford to test at, and that conviction that the virtual world genuinely sharpens real driving has stayed with him. He's a Ferrari Esports driver and was deeply involved in the development of Assetto Corsa Competizione, contributing to the audio, the track accuracy, and the feel of the 488. When the pandemic put motorsport on hold, he turned that experience into something bigger and founded both SimGrid, a community platform for organised online racing, and Coach Dave Academy, which produces professional setups, driver coaching, and engineering sessions for sim racers of every level.

Off the track, Perel has built one of the most respected voices in the sim racing world through his YouTube channel, where he publishes weekly lap guides, race tutorials, and live streams that pull back the curtain on what a working pro actually thinks about behind the wheel. The throughline across all of it, the real racing, the coaching business, the videos, is the same idea he stumbled into as a teenager with a PlayStation: that real and virtual racing belong to the same craft, and getting better at one will make you better at the other.

featured.name: Alex Catlow
featured.description:
Alex Catlow is a 17-year-old racing driver competing in his first season of car racing with Bromley Motorsport in the BRSCC Mazda Clubman Championship. It's a grid stacked with seasoned veterans on budgets he can't match, but that's exactly the point. He's there to find out where he stands, with the Rookie title firmly in his sights.

His route in has been slightly unconventional. Alex moved out of karts only two years ago, having come up through Club100, the UK's leading arrive-and-drive Rotax series, where close racing taught him how to fight in tight fields. Before that, the real foundation was built in the sim, where he could learn circuits and put in the repetitions that real-world testing would never have allowed.
featured.descriptionTwo:
Racing is the focus, but not the whole picture. Alongside competing, Alex is training as a motorsport mechanic to understand the cars he drives from the ground up, and works as a photographer when the schedule allows. Both feed back into his driving in ways he didn't expect when he started.

The long-term goal is a sustainable career in motorsport, both as a driver and as a coach helping the next generation find their way into the sport. Follow along as he chases the Rookie Championship and works towards turning all of this into something that lasts.

featured.name: Alex Berg
featured.description:
Born in Calgary, Alberta, Alex Berg was immersed in motorsport from day one, following in the footsteps of his father, 1986 Formula One driver Allen Berg. After six years in French immersion school, he’s fluent in English and Spanish and now makes his home in Buford, Georgia.

In 2020, Alex was named one of North America’s top 18 kart racers by eKartingNews.com, earning a Mazda Motorsports USA Factory Team Scholarship Shootout berth. He went on to drive for the Mazda Factory Team in the 2021 Spec MX-5 Challenge, finishing runner-up in the championship with three wins and five podiums.
featured.descriptionTwo:
Late in 2023, Alex claimed a Team Canada Scholarship and represented his country at the Formula Ford Festival and Walter Hayes Trophy at Silverstone and Brands Hatch—an honor that underscored his rising international profile.

Hired in November 2022 as Ligier Automotive's designated test driver, Alex was the first ever to pilot the new JSF4 22 ahead of its 2024 F4 U.S. Championship debut. In 2024 he raced the #48 Fortec Motorsports Tatuus T4-21 in the F4 British Championship, scoring multiple podiums and victories. For 2026 he'll contest the USF Juniors Presented by Continental Tire championship with Berg Racing, piloting the #68 Tatuus JR-23 as the team - founded by his father, ex-F1 driver Allen Berg - makes its debut on the USF Pro Championships ladder, the first step on the scholarship-funded pathway to the NTT IndyCar Series and the Indianapolis 500.

featured.name: Barnaby Brewer
featured.description:
From modest beginnings on a Logitech G920 through hours a day on his Sim rig, Barnaby Brewer now competes online with the world's best and on track in the UK's 2026 Ford Fiesta Junior Championship.

Having learnt everything about racing from the sim, he races with Phoenix Sim Racing, a team that promotes drivers with different abilities. Barnaby has competed in Jimmy Broadbent's Race for Mental Health and various other IRacing special events.

On track, Barnaby is part of the Race Car Consultants' team, competing with 30 other 14-17 year olds in a race homologated Ford Fiesta Mark 7 with a 1.6 Zetec engine.
featured.descriptionTwo:
Having struggled with mental health challenges throughout his life, Barnaby is racing for YoungMinds for his first season in real racing. It's the UK’s leading charity fighting for a world where no young person feels alone with their mental health. To help raise money, Barnaby is currently running a fundraiser where the top 100 donators' messages will be handwritten and placed on the back of his car. Donors can also request challenges or dares that Barnaby will complete as part of his Nürburgring series. 

Barnaby's story - neurodiversity, motorsport ambition, and mental health advocacy - helped him develop a high-trust online audience with over 3 million views in just 6 months. Engagement rates with his @payattentionbarnaby socials of over 11% outperform industry benchmarks because they are driven by content that is real and not manufactured.

Barnaby is currently searching for sponsors, so if you're interested, please do take a look at his deal room

featured.name: Media Friends
featured.description:
Put your motorsport event on the map with Media Friends, the UAE’s go-to motorsport media and broadcasting partner. We make sure your races are seen, remembered, and talked about—locally, regionally, and worldwide. 

From lights out to the chequered flag, we deliver broadcast-quality live streaming that matches the standards of major international sports. Multi-camera coverage, professional commentary, live timing, and instant replays turn every race into a premium viewing experience.
featured.descriptionTwo:
But we don’t stop when the race ends. Media Friends creates polished highlights, promotional videos, and real-time social content that keeps fans engaged long after race day—and gives sponsors content that delivers real value.

We also help drivers and teams build their brands beyond the track. From race-weekend content to custom highlight reels and season recaps, we give competitors the media tools they need to stand out to fans and sponsors alike.

Trusted by leading championships and led by decades of motorsport media experience, Media Friends doesn’t pitch potential—we deliver results. If you want your event to look world-class and reach the audience it deserves, let’s talk.

featured.name: Pass Pursuits
featured.description:
Since 2005, Pass Pursuits has been curating extraordinary road trips that celebrate the pure joy of driving amidst Europe’s most breathtaking landscapes. With 20 years of expertise and a deep passion for the art of the journey, we are excited to share our privately crafted experiences with driving enthusiasts who crave adventure, luxury, and the thrill of the open road.

Our signature road trips are designed to highlight Europe’s most iconic driving routes, with the Alps as our crown jewel. Picture yourself navigating the hairpin turns of the Stelvio Pass, cruising along the serpentine Großglockner High Alpine Road, and marveling at the awe-inspiring vistas of the Col de Turini. These legendary roads, combined with our attention to detail, create an unforgettable driving experience that caters to your passion for motoring.
Each of our road trips is meticulously hand crafted to ensure a seamless blend of luxury, adventure, and culture. From the rolling hills of the Black Forest to the majestic peaks of the Alps, our journeys are tailored to the car enthusiast. Whether it’s the hairpin bends of the Stelvio Pass, or Flugplatz at the Nurburgring, we organise everything so you can focus on the joy of the drive.
featured.descriptionTwo:
But the journey doesn’t end with spectacular roads. In 2025, we are taking our adventures to new heights with an exclusive 10-day expedition. Drive through the heart of Europe’s majestic mountain ranges, revel in the pristine beauty of the Alpine passes, and immerse yourself in the unparalleled thrill of the drive. This incredible journey culminates in an adrenaline-fueled finale at Brands Hatch, where you’ll take the wheel of a purebred Ginetta G56 race car for a private track day. Feel the power, precision, and exhilaration of this legendary machine on one of Britain’s most iconic circuits.

At Pass Pursuits, we believe every aspect of your trip should be extraordinary. From handpicked luxury accommodations to exquisite dining and expert guidance, we ensure that every moment complements the adventure, leaving you free to enjoy the drive of a lifetime.

Join us for a journey that combines the world’s greatest driving roads with the unmatched thrill of track time in a Ginetta G56. This is not just a road trip—it’s the ultimate driving experience. In 2025, we’re bringing you our most unique and exhilarating adventure yet—a 10-day journey through the mountains of Europe, culminating in an unforgettable finale at Brands Hatch, where you’ll drive purebred Ginetta G56 race cars on a private track day.

featured.name: MiddleGround Motorsports
featured.description:
MiddleGround is a dynamic motorsport-focused media and consulting company dedicated to uplifting and amplifying the voices of young racing talents. Founded in September of last year, 2025, MiddleGround was created with the mission of providing a platform where aspiring drivers from around the world can share their stories, freely and authentically, gaining recognition beyond the track. Since last year, MiddleGround successfully featured more than 50 young drivers across various junior motorsport categories, helping them tell their journeys in a way that resonates with fans, teams, and industry professionals.

From the outset, MiddleGround has embraced collaboration as a core value. The company has established strategic partnerships with organizations such as Aquila Formula 1000, We Racing, JS Trackside Media, Per4m Mindset, and The Racing Line. These partnerships work cohesively to amplify the narratives of emerging talent, celebrating milestones, capturing pivotal moments, and sharing these stories with broader motorsport audiences. Through this network of partners, MiddleGround has broadened its reach, creating vibrant connections that support visibility and recognition for young racers at different stages of their careers.
featured.descriptionTwo:
In addition to its media presence, MiddleGround has carved a niche in motorsport research and analytics. The company prides itself on producing thorough research and case studies tailored for racing teams, offering valuable insights into progress tracking, performance narratives, and competitive development. Complementing this analytical work is MiddleGround’s consultancy service, which supports drivers in preparing for professional opportunities. This includes crafting sponsorship pitch decks, refining personal branding, evaluating drivers’ market value, assessing potential ROI, and offering strategic guidance to strengthen drivers’ outreach efforts.

Beyond performance and storytelling, MiddleGround positions itself as an inclusive and socially conscious organization. The company actively welcomes perspectives from neurodiverse drivers and motorsport professionals, championing inclusion through initiatives such as their 1 Race 4 Autism+ program. By fostering a supportive environment for diverse voices, MiddleGround continues to expand the narrative of motorsport, one that celebrates talent, diversity, and the power of every driver’s story.

featured.name: SLICKS
featured.description:
SLICKS was born to reignite the flame of physical media in motorsport and automotive content. A flame that’s been dying out.

It’s a magazine that speaks to the current generation of fans. A magazine that isn’t top to tail with flashy watch advertisements and pretentious aftershaves. 

SLICKS takes the passion of the people and puts it back on a page. 

For car lovers, bike enthusiasts, motorsport nutters, SLICKS is for you.
featured.descriptionTwo:
For young creatives and talented pros:

SLICKS is the place for you to strut your stuff. Every issue will be crammed full of independent talent, telling your motorsport story your way. You have full creative freedom on what you produce for SLICKS (not many publications will give you that).

For fans of automotive entertainment:

SLICKS will be the most diverse collection of stories in the industry. We want creative professionals to see SLICKS as the home of quality storytelling. No corporate intervention. No butchering of the narrative in post-editing. Raw, pure, honest storytelling from independent voices. 

For teams, series and organisations:

SLICKS will be your portal into the next generation of automotive and motorsport talent. When you read SLICKS, you’re supporting independent creatives, and there’s a good chance you'll see someone unfamiliar. We’re making SLICKS the place to find your team’s next press officer, photographer, copywriter, graphic designer, you name it. 

Treat SLICKS as a Yellow Pages-esque publication. A hub. A pool.

That’s the story of SLICKS. Now it’s time to tell yours.

featured.name: COG Racing Simulators
featured.description: At Come Over Gaming, we specialize in driving simulators for Simracing. We have used our expertise to create top-quality static and motion simulators. All our products are compatible with all existing simulation platforms on the market without any limitations. No matter what your needs or budget may be, we have a solution to help you experience the most realistic and immersive simulation experience possible.

featured.name: Copynthat
featured.description:
I'm Lewis, founder of copynthat, the alternative answer to motorsport copywriting, marketing and communications. 

If you’ve had enough of the bland, corporate shite that teams are spewing out with no love for their written content, copynthat is here to change that. 

Teams are starting to see the light and recognise the value of engaging written content. Producing copywriting for fans, sponsors and investors creates a new layer of ownership and loyalty. 

And what do loyal fans do? Spend money, wear merch and spread the word about their favourite team.
featured.descriptionTwo:
In 2024, smaller, national series and teams figured it was time to shake up their content and took me on board to give them a much-needed content boost. I started to cut about the paddocks of the UK's finest circuits delivering on-site copywriting, reporting and more. You’ve probably seen this crap haircut circulating your paddock. featured.description:
One application. One chance. A lifetime opportunity.

Join women worldwide and take your first step into motorsport.

For just £99, take your first step into a global competition designed for women with little to no racing experience. Attend an assessment day, prove your potential, and fight for the chance to reach the world finals on Sweden’s legendary ice. Apply below.
featured.descriptionTwo:
The Racing Women Global Competition is our worldwide search for natural racing talent. It's open to any girl or woman 13 or older. No previous racing experience is required, no matter their nationality or background. If they have the ambition, determination, and instinct behind the wheel, they are eligible to apply.

Each applicant attends a 1-day assessment day in one of the assessment regions (UK, UAE, India), where they'll be tested across five areas used in real motorsport development programmes:

- Karting – assessing speed, racecraft, and natural driving ability
- Reactions – critical cognitive testing
- Fitness – baseline endurance and capability
- Media Test – on-camera confidence and communication
- Written Assessment – racing knowledge, awareness, passion and motivation

featured.name: Racing Mind Strategies
featured.description:
Performance Psychology for Sim & Race Car Drivers

Racing Mind Strategies™ provides elite performance psychology for drivers who compete under pressure - whether in the simulator or on track.

I am Dr Nick Molinaro, and I am licensed psychologist with more than four decades of experience helping high-performance individuals excel when consequences are real. My work spans clinical psychology, elite sport, and motorsport, grounded in neuroscience, validated assessment, and lived racing experience. I work with athletes across collegiate, professional, World Class, and Olympic sport, as well as drivers competing at the highest levels of motorsport.

In racing, I previously served as Director of Psychological Performance for Racing Prodigy, supporting the transition of elite sim racers into real-world race cars. I currently serve as Director of Psychological Performance for Matrix Motorsports, working with drivers competing in IGT, PCA, SRO, IMSA, Mustang Challenge, and related professional series.

Motorsport is not just physical. It is cognitive, emotional, and attentional. The margin between performance and error is often determined by how a driver manages pressure, attention, and decision-making under load.

Why Motorsport Psychology Matters - for Every Driver

At speed, performance is limited less by effort and more by the brain’s ability to process information under stress. The best drivers are not simply faster—they are calmer, clearer, and more decisive when it matters most.

Formula 1 drivers consistently emphasize this reality. Drivers such as Nico Rosberg, Lewis Hamilton, and Lando Norris have spoken openly about the role of mindset, pressure regulation, and mental preparation in championship-level performance. At the highest level of motorsport, races—and titles—are often decided not by raw pace, but by who manages pressure, recovers from mistakes, and sustains focus over time.

This is why motorsport psychology is no longer optional. It is a performance requirement.
featured.descriptionTwo:
What Racing Mind Strategies™ Delivers

Racing Mind Strategies™ develops the mental skills that directly influence results:

• Composure under pressure
• Rapid recovery after mistakes
• Sustained focus over time
• Emotional regulation under consequence
• Clear, decisive thinking at speed

Drivers learn how to stabilize arousal, protect attentional bandwidth, and execute clean decisions—whether managing traffic, restarts, degradation, strategy uncertainty, or championship pressure.

High-Stakes Assessment & Individualized Training

Drivers may complete validated psychological assessments originally developed for high-risk operational environments, including tools used with U.S. Special Forces programs (including Navy SEALs) and NASA research. These assessments measure how individuals perform under pressure, manage cognitive load, and make decisions in time-critical situations.

Each assessed driver receives a personalized psychological performance profile identifying performance strengths and potential interference factors. This profile can be applied to any track, any turn, and any race weekend, translating data into:

• Clear performance goals
• Targeted mental skills
• Practical race-weekend strategies

Training is objective, individualized, and measurable.

Built for Sim Drivers and Race Car Drivers

Whether you are:

• a sim driver preparing for real-world competition
• a developing racer building foundations
• or a professional driver refining elite performance

Racing Mind Strategies™ provides individualized coaching, structured development programs, race-weekend preparation protocols, and focused debriefing strategies—designed to help drivers access a calmer, clearer, and more instinctive state of performance.

When pressure is highest, the mind determines the outcome.

Racing Mind Strategies™
Performance when it matters most.
